<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title5"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-3.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 3.2

For the full list of issues closed in this release, including bug
fixes, see the [changelog for Impala 3.2](https://impala.apache.org/docs/changelog-3.2.html).

The following is a list of noteworthy issues fixed in Impala 3.2:

- [IMPALA-341](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-341) - Remote profiles
  are no longer ignored by the coordinator for the queries with the
  `LIMIT` clause.
- [IMPALA-941](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-941)- Impala supports
  fully qualified table names that start with a number.
- [IMPALA-1048](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1048) - The query
  execution summary now includes the total time taken and memory
  consumed by the data sink at the root of each query fragment.
- [IMPALA-3323](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3323) - Fixed the issue
  where valid impala-shell options, such as
  `--ldap_password_cmd`, were unrecognized when the
  `--config_file` option was specified.
- [IMPALA-5397](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-5397) - If a query has a
  dedicated coordinator, its end time is now set when the query releases
  its admission control resources. With no dedicated coordinator, the
  end time is set on un-registration.
- [IMPALA-5474](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-5474) - Fixed an issue
  where adding a trivial subquery to a query with an error turns the
  error into a warning.
- [IMPALA-6521](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-6521) - When set,
  experimental flags are now shown in /varz in web UI and log
  files.
- [IMPALA-6900](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-6900) -
  `INVALIDATE METADATA` operation is no longer ignored
  when HMS is empty.
- [IMPALA-7446](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7446) - Impala enables
  buffer pool garbage collection when near process memory limit to
  prevent queries from spilling to disk earlier than necessary.
- [IMPALA-7659](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7659) - In
  `COMPUTE STATS`, Impala counts the number of
  `NULL` values in a table
- [IMPALA-7857](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7857) - Logs more
  information about StateStore failure detection.
- [IMPALA-7928](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7928) - To increase the
  efficiency of the HDFS file handle cache, remote reads for a
  particular file are scheduled to a consistent set of executor nodes.
- [IMPALA-7929](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7929) - Impala query on
  tables created via Hive and mapped to HBase failed with an internal
  exception because the qualifier of the HBase key column is null in the
  mapped table. Impala relaxed the requirement and allows a
  `NULL` qualifier.
- [IMPALA-7960](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7960) - Impala now
  returns a correct result when comparing `TIMESTAMP` to
  a string literal in a binary predicate where the
  `TIMESTAMP` is casted to `VARCHAR` of
  smaller length.
- [IMPALA-7961](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-7961) - Fixed an issue
  where queries running with the `SYNC_DDL` query option
  can fail when the Catalog Server is under a heavy load with concurrent
  catalog operations of long-running DDLs.
- [IMPALA-8026](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8026) - Impala query
  profile now reports correct row counts for all nested loop join
  modes.
- [IMPALA-8061](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8061) - Impala correctly
  initializes `S3_ACCESS_VALIDATED` variable to zero
  when `TARGET_FILESYSTEM=s3`.
- [IMPALA-8154](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8154) - Disabled the
  Kerberos `auth_to_local` setting to prevent
  connection issues between `impalads`.
- [IMPALA-8188](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8188) - Impala now
  correctly detects an NVME device name and handles it.
- [IMPALA-8245](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8245) - Added hostname
  to the timeout error message to enable the user to easily identify the
  host which has reached a bad connection state with the HDFS
  NameNode.
- [IMPALA-8254](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-8254) - `COMPUTE
  STATS` failed if `COMPRESSION_CODEC` is
  set.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.6.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.6.0

The following list contains the most critical fixed issues
(`priority='Blocker'`) from the JIRA system.
For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.6.0, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=%20type%20%3D%20bug%20and%20project%20%3D%20IMPALA%20AND%20resolution%20%3D%20fixed%20AND%20affectedVersion%20!%3D%20%22Impala%202.6.0%22%20AND%20fixVersion%20%3D%20%22Impala%202.6.0%22%20and%20not%20labels%20%3D%20broken-build%20order%20by%20priority%20desc).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title17"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--runtimestate::error_log_-crashes"></a>

### RuntimeState::error\_log\_ crashes

A crash could occur, with stack trace pointing to `impala::RuntimeState::ErrorLog`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3385](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3385)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title18"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hiveudfcall::open-produces-unsynchronized-access-to-jniutil::global_refs_-vector"></a>

### HiveUdfCall::Open() produces unsynchronized access to JniUtil::global\_refs\_ vector

A crash could occur because of contention between multiple calls to Java UDFs.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3378](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3378)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title19"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hbasetablewriter::createputlist-produces-unsynchronized-access-to-jniutil::global_refs_-vector"></a>

### HBaseTableWriter::CreatePutList() produces unsynchronized access to JniUtil::global\_refs\_ vector

A crash could occur because of contention between multiple concurrent statements writing to HBase.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3379](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3379)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title20"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--stress-test-failure:-sorter.cc:745-check-failed:-i-0-1-vs.-0"></a>

### Stress test failure: sorter.cc:745] Check failed: i == 0 (1 vs. 0)

A crash or wrong results could occur if the spill-to-disk mechanism encountered a zero-length string at
the very end of a data block.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3317](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3317)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title21"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--string-data-coming-out-of-agg-can-be-corrupted-by-blocking-operators"></a>

### String data coming out of agg can be corrupted by blocking operators

If a query plan contains an aggregation node producing string values anywhere within a subplan
(that is,if in the SQL statement, the aggregate function appears within an inline view over a collection column), the results of the aggregation may be incorrect.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3311](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3311)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title22"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ctas-with-subquery-throws-authzexception"></a>

### CTAS with subquery throws AuthzException

A `CREATE TABLE AS SELECT` operation could fail with an authorization error, due to a slight difference in the privilege checking for the CTAS operation.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3269](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3269)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title23"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--crash-on-inserting-into-table-with-binary-and-parquet"></a>

### Crash on inserting into table with binary and parquet

Impala incorrectly allowed `BINARY` to be specified as a column type, resulting in a crash during a write to a Parquet table with a column of that type.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3237](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3237)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title24"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--rowbatch::maxtuplebuffersize-calculation-incorrect-may-lead-to-memory-corruption"></a>

### RowBatch::MaxTupleBufferSize() calculation incorrect, may lead to memory corruption

A crash could occur while querying tables with very large rows, for example wide tables with many
columns or very large string values. This problem was identified in Impala 2.3, but had low
reproducibility in subsequent releases. The fix ensures the memory allocation size is correct.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3105](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3105)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title25"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--thrift-buffer-overflows-when-serialize-more-than-3355443200-bytes-in-impala"></a>

### Thrift buffer overflows when serialize more than 3355443200 bytes in impala

A very large memory allocation within the catalogd daemon could exceed an internal Thrift limit, causing a crash.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3494](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3494)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title26"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--altering-table-partition-s-storage-format-is-not-working-and-crashing-the-daemon"></a>

### Altering table partition's storage format is not working and crashing the daemon

If a partitioned table used a file format other than Avro, and the file format of an individual partition
was changed to Avro, subsequent queries could encounter a crash.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3314](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3314)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title27"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--race-condition-may-cause-scanners-to-spin-with-runtime-filters-on-avro-or-sequence-files"></a>

### Race condition may cause scanners to spin with runtime filters on Avro or Sequence files

A timing problem during runtime filter processing could cause queries against Avro or SequenceFile tables
to hang.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3798](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3798)

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title28"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.5.4"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.5.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.5.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.5.2

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title30"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.5.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.5.1

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title31"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.5.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.5.0

The following list contains the most critical issues (`priority='Blocker'`) from the JIRA system.
For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.5, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=%20type%20%3D%20bug%20and%20project%20%3D%20IMPALA%20AND%20resolution%20%3D%20fixed%20AND%20affectedVersion%20!%3D%20%22Impala%202.5.0%22%20AND%20fixVersion%20%3D%20%22Impala%202.5.0%22%20and%20not%20labels%20%3D%20broken-build%20order%20by%20priority%20desc).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title32"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--stress-test-hit-assert-in-llvm:-external-function-could-not-be-resolved"></a>

### Stress test hit assert in LLVM: external function could not be resolved

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2683](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2683)

The stress test was running a build with the TPC-H, TPC-DS, and TPC-H nested queries with scale factor 3.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title33"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-is-crashing-if-udf-jar-is-not-available-in-hdfs-location-for-first-time"></a>

### Impalad is crashing if udf jar is not available in hdfs location for first time

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2365](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2365)

If a UDF JAR was not available in the HDFS location specified in the `CREATE FUNCTION` statement, the impalad daemon could crash.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title34"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--pagg-hits-mem_limit-when-switching-to-i-o-buffers"></a>

### PAGG hits mem\_limit when switching to I/O buffers

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2535](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2535)

A join query could fail with an out-of-memory error despite the apparent presence of sufficient memory.
The cause was the internal ordering of operations that could cause a later phase of the query to
allocate memory required by an earlier phase of the query. The workaround was to either increase
or decrease the `MEM_LIMIT` query option, because the issue would only occur for a specific
combination of memory limit and data volume.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title35"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--prevent-migrating-incorrectly-inferred-identity-predicates-into-inline-views"></a>

### Prevent migrating incorrectly inferred identity predicates into inline views

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2643](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2643)

Referring to the same column twice in a view definition could cause the view to omit
rows where that column contained a `NULL` value. This could cause
incorrect results due to an inaccurate `COUNT(*)` value or rows missing
from the result set.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title36"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-migration-assignment-of-on-clause-predicates-inside-inline-views"></a>

### Fix migration/assignment of On-clause predicates inside inline views

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1459](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1459)

Some combinations of `ON` clauses in join queries could result in comparisons
being applied at the wrong stage of query processing, leading to incorrect results.
Wrong predicate assignment could happen under the following conditions:

- The query includes an inline view that contains an outer join.
- That inline view is joined with another table in the enclosing query block.
- That join has an `ON` clause containing a predicate that
  only references columns originating from the outer-joined tables inside the inline view.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title37"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--wrong-plan-of-not-in-aggregate-subquery-when-a-constant-is-used-in-subquery-predicate"></a>

### Wrong plan of NOT IN aggregate subquery when a constant is used in subquery predicate

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2093](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2093)

`IN` subqueries might return wrong results if the left-hand side of the `IN` is a constant.
For example:

```

select * from alltypestiny t1
  where 10 not in (select sum(int_col) from alltypestiny);
```

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title38"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--parquet-dictdecoders-accumulate-throughout-query"></a>

### Parquet DictDecoders accumulate throughout query

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2940](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2940)

Parquet dictionary decoders can accumulate throughout query execution, leading to excessive memory usage. One decoder is created per-column per-split.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title39"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--planner-doesn-t-set-the-has_local_target-field-correctly"></a>

### Planner doesn't set the has\_local\_target field correctly

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3056](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3056)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title40"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--mempool-allocation-growth-behavior"></a>

### MemPool allocation growth behavior

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2742](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2742)

Currently, the MemPool would always double the size of the last allocation.
This can lead to bad behavior if the MemPool transferred the ownership of all its data
except the last chunk. In the next allocation, the next allocated chunk would double
the size of this large chunk, which can be undesirable.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title41"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--drop-partition-operations-don-t-follow-the-catalog-s-locking-protocol"></a>

### Drop partition operations don't follow the catalog's locking protocol

**Bug:** [IMPALA-3035](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-3035)

The `CatalogOpExecutor.alterTableDropPartition()` function violates
the locking protocol used in the catalog that requires `catalogLock_`
to be acquired before any table-level lock. That may cause deadlocks when `ALTER TABLE DROP PARTITION`
is executed concurrently with other DDL operations.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title42"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--having-clause-without-aggregation-not-applied-properly"></a>

### HAVING clause without aggregation not applied properly

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2215](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2215)

A query with a `HAVING` clause but no `GROUP BY` clause was not being rejected, despite being invalid syntax. For example:

```

select case when 1=1 then 'didit' end as c1 from (select 1 as one) a having 1!=1;
```

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title43"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hit-dcheck-check-failed:-hasdateortime"></a>

### Hit DCHECK Check failed: HasDateOrTime()

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2914](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2914)

`TimestampValue::ToTimestampVal()` requires a valid `TimestampValue` as input.
This requirement was not enforced in some places, leading to serious errors.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title44"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--aggregation-spill-loop-gives-up-too-early-leading-to-mem-limit-exceeded-errors"></a>

### Aggregation spill loop gives up too early leading to mem limit exceeded errors

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2986](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2986)

An aggregation query could fail with an out-of-memory error, despite sufficient memory being reported as available.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title45"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--datastreamsender::channel::closeinternal-does-not-close-the-channel-on-an-error."></a>

### DataStreamSender::Channel::CloseInternal() does not close the channel on an error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2592](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2592)

Some queries do not close an internal communication channel on an error.
This will cause the node on the other side of the channel to wait indefinitely, causing the query to hang.
For example, this issue could happen on a Kerberos-enabled system if the credential cache was outdated.
Although the affected query hangs, the impalad daemons continue processing other queries.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title46"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--codegen-does-not-catch-exceptions-in-from_unixtime"></a>

### Codegen does not catch exceptions in FROM\_UNIXTIME()

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2184](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2184)

Querying for the min or max value of a timestamp cast from a bigint via `from_unixtime()`
fails silently and crashes instances of impalad when the input includes a value outside of the valid range.

**Workaround:** Disable native code generation with:

```

SET disable_codegen=true;
```

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title47"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-returns-wrong-result-for-function-conv-bigint-from_base-to_base"></a>

### Impala returns wrong result for function 'conv(bigint, from\_base, to\_base)'

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2788](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2788)

Impala returns wrong result for function `conv()`.
Function `conv(bigint, from_base, to_base)` returns an correct result, while `conv(string, from_base, to_base)` returns the correct value.
For example:

```
select 2061013007, conv(2061013007, 16, 10), conv('2061013007', 16, 10); +------------+--------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | conv(2061013007, 16, 10) | conv('2061013007', 16, 10) | +------------+--------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | 1627467783               | 139066421255               | +------------+--------------------------+----------------------------+ Fetched 1 row(s) in 0.65s
select 2061013007, conv(cast(2061013007 as bigint), 16, 10), conv('2061013007', 16, 10); +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | conv(cast(2061013007 as bigint), 16, 10) | conv('2061013007', 16, 10) | +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | 1627467783                               | 139066421255               | +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+
select 2061013007, conv(cast(2061013007 as string), 16, 10), conv('2061013007', 16, 10); +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | conv(cast(2061013007 as string), 16, 10) | conv('2061013007', 16, 10) | +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | 139066421255                             | 139066421255               | +------------+------------------------------------------+----------------------------+
select 2061013007, conv(cast(cast(2061013007 as decimal(20,0)) as bigint), 16, 10), conv('2061013007', 16, 10); +------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | conv(cast(cast(2061013007 as decimal(20,0)) as bigint), 16, 10) | conv('2061013007', 16, 10) | +------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | 2061013007 | 1627467783                                                      | 139066421255               | +------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+
```

**Workaround:**
Cast the value to string and use `conv(string, from_base, to_base)` for conversion.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title48"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.4.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.4.1

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title49"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.4.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.4.0

The set of fixes for Impala in Impala 2.4.0 is the same as
in Impala 2.3.2.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title50"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.3.4"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.3.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title51"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.3.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.3.2

This section lists the most serious or frequently encountered customer
issues fixed in Impala 2.3.2.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title52"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--segv-in-analyticevalnode-touching-null-input_stream_"></a>

### SEGV in AnalyticEvalNode touching NULL input\_stream\_

A query involving an analytic function could encounter a serious error.
This issue was encountered infrequently, depending upon specific combinations
of queries and data.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2829](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2829)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title53"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--free-local-allocations-per-row-batch-in-non-partitioned-agg-and-hj"></a>

### Free local allocations per row batch in non-partitioned AGG and HJ

An outer join query could fail unexpectedly with an out-of-memory error
when the "spill to disk" mechanism was turned off.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2722](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2722)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title54"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--free-local-allocations-once-for-every-row-batch-when-building-hash-tables"></a>

### Free local allocations once for every row batch when building hash tables

A join query could encounter a serious error due to an internal failure to allocate memory, which
resulted in dereferencing a `NULL` pointer.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2612](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2612)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title55"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--prevent-migrating-incorrectly-inferred-identity-predicates-into-inline-views-2"></a>

### Prevent migrating incorrectly inferred identity predicates into inline views

Referring to the same column twice in a view definition could cause the view to omit
rows where that column contained a `NULL` value. This could cause
incorrect results due to an inaccurate `COUNT(*)` value or rows missing
from the result set.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2643](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2643)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title56"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-grants-on-uris-with-uppercase-letters"></a>

### Fix GRANTs on URIs with uppercase letters

A `GRANT` statement for a URI could be ineffective if the URI
contained uppercase letters, for example in an uppercase directory name.
Subsequent statements, such as `CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE`
with a `LOCATION` clause, could fail with an authorization exception.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2695](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2695)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-2664-552__impala-2648-552"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-sending-large-partition-stats-objects-over-thrift"></a>

### Avoid sending large partition stats objects over thrift

The catalogd daemon could encounter a serious error
when loading the incremental statistics metadata for tables with large
numbers of partitions and columns. The problem occurred when the
internal representation of metadata for the table exceeded 2
GB, for example in a table with 20K partitions and 77 columns. The fix causes a
`COMPUTE INCREMENTAL STATS` operation to fail if it
would produce metadata that exceeded the maximum size.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2664](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2664), [IMPALA-2648](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2648)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title58"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--throw-analysiserror-if-table-properties-are-too-large-for-the-hive-metastore"></a>

### Throw AnalysisError if table properties are too large (for the Hive metastore)

`CREATE TABLE` or `ALTER TABLE` statements could fail with
metastore database errors due to length limits on the `SERDEPROPERTIES` and `TBLPROPERTIES` clauses.
(The limit on key size is 256, while the limit on value size is 4000.) The fix makes Impala handle these error conditions
more cleanly, by detecting too-long values rather than passing them to the metastore database.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2226](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2226)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title59"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--make-max_page_header_size-configurable"></a>

### Make MAX\_PAGE\_HEADER\_SIZE configurable

Impala could fail to access Parquet data files with page headers larger than 8 MB, which could
occur, for example, if the minimum or maximum values for a column were long strings. The
fix adds a configuration setting `--max_page_header_size`, which you can use to
increase the Impala size limit to a value higher than 8 MB.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2273](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2273)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title60"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--reduce-scanner-memory-usage"></a>

### reduce scanner memory usage

Queries on Parquet tables could consume excessive memory (potentially multiple gigabytes) due to producing
large intermediate data values while evaluating groups of rows. The workaround was to reduce the size of
the `NUM_SCANNER_THREADS` query option, the `BATCH_SIZE` query option, or both.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2473](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2473)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title61"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--handle-error-when-distinct-and-aggregates-are-used-with-a-having-clause"></a>

### Handle error when distinct and aggregates are used with a having clause

A query that included a `DISTINCT` operator and a `HAVING` clause, but no
aggregate functions or `GROUP BY`, would fail with an uninformative error message.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2113](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2113)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title62"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--handle-error-when-star-based-select-item-and-aggregate-are-incorrectly-used"></a>

### Handle error when star based select item and aggregate are incorrectly used

A query that included `*` in the `SELECT` list, in addition to an
aggregate function call, would fail with an uninformative message if the query had no
`GROUP BY` clause.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2225](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2225)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title63"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--refactor-mempool-usage-in-hbase-scan-node"></a>

### Refactor MemPool usage in HBase scan node

Queries involving HBase tables used substantially more memory than in earlier Impala versions.
The problem occurred starting in Impala 2.2.8, as a result of the changes for IMPALA-2284.
The fix for this issue involves removing a separate memory work area for HBase queries
and reusing other memory that was already allocated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2731](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2731)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title64"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-migration-assignment-of-on-clause-predicates-inside-inline-views-2"></a>

### Fix migration/assignment of On-clause predicates inside inline views

Some combinations of `ON` clauses in join queries could result in comparisons
being applied at the wrong stage of query processing, leading to incorrect results.
Wrong predicate assignment could happen under the following conditions:

- The query includes an inline view that contains an outer join.
- That inline view is joined with another table in the enclosing query block.
- That join has an `ON` clause containing a predicate that
  only references columns originating from the outer-joined tables inside the inline view.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1459](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1459)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title65"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--dcheck-in-parquet-scanner-after-block-read-error"></a>

### DCHECK in parquet scanner after block read error

A debug build of Impala could encounter a serious error after encountering some kinds of I/O
errors for Parquet files. This issue only occurred in debug builds, not release builds.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2558](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2558)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title66"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--pagg-hits-mem_limit-when-switching-to-i-o-buffers-2"></a>

### PAGG hits mem\_limit when switching to I/O buffers

A join query could fail with an out-of-memory error despite the apparent presence of sufficient memory.
The cause was the internal ordering of operations that could cause a later phase of the query to
allocate memory required by an earlier phase of the query. The workaround was to either increase
or decrease the `MEM_LIMIT` query option, because the issue would only occur for a specific
combination of memory limit and data volume.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2535](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2535)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title67"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-check-failed:-sorter_runs_.back-is_pinned_"></a>

### Fix check failed: sorter\_runs\_.back()->is\_pinned\_

A query could fail with an internal error while calculating the memory limit.
This was an infrequent condition uncovered during stress testing.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2559](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2559)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title68"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--don-t-ignore-status-returned-by-datastreamrecvr::createmerger"></a>

### Don't ignore Status returned by DataStreamRecvr::CreateMerger()

A query could fail with an internal error while calculating the memory limit.
This was an infrequent condition uncovered during stress testing.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2614](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2614), [IMPALA-2559](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2559)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title69"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--datastreamsender::send-does-not-return-an-error-status-if-sendbatch-failed"></a>

### DataStreamSender::Send() does not return an error status if SendBatch() failed

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2591](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2591)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title70"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--re-enable-ssl-and-kerberos-on-server-server"></a>

### Re-enable SSL and Kerberos on server-server

These fixes lift the restriction on using SSL encryption and Kerberos authentication together
for internal communication between Impala components.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2598](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2598), [IMPALA-2747](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2747)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title71"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.3.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.3.1

The version of Impala that is included with Impala 2.3.1 is identical
to Impala 2.3.0. There are no new bug fixes, new features, or
incompatible changes.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title72"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.3.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.3.0

This section lists the most serious or frequently encountered customer
issues fixed in Impala 2.3. Any issues already fixed in
Impala 2.2 maintenance releases (up through Impala 2.2.8) are also included.
Those issues are listed under the respective Impala 2.2 sections and are
not repeated here.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title73"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fixes-for-serious-errors"></a>

### Fixes for Serious Errors

A number of issues were resolved that could result in serious errors
when encountered. The most critical or commonly encountered are
listed here.

**Bugs:**
[IMPALA-2168](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2168), [IMPALA-2378](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2378), [IMPALA-2369](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2369), [IMPALA-2357](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2357), [IMPALA-2319](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2319), [IMPALA-2314](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2314), [IMPALA-2016](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2016)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title74"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fixes-for-correctness-errors"></a>

### Fixes for Correctness Errors

A number of issues were resolved that could result in wrong results
when encountered. The most critical or commonly encountered are
listed here.

**Bugs:**
[IMPALA-2192](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2192), [IMPALA-2440](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2440), [IMPALA-2090](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2090), [IMPALA-2086](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2086), [IMPALA-1947](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1947), [IMPALA-1917](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1917)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title75"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.10"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.10

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title76"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.9"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.9

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.9.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title77"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-return-empty-result-if-it-contains-nullliteral-in-inlineview"></a>

### Query return empty result if it contains NullLiteral in inlineview

If an inline view in a `FROM` clause contained a `NULL` literal, the result set was empty.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1917](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1917)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title78"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hbase-scan-node-uses-2-4x-memory-after-upgrade-to-impala-2.2.8"></a>

### HBase scan node uses 2-4x memory after upgrade to Impala 2.2.8

Queries involving HBase tables used substantially more memory than in earlier Impala versions.
The problem occurred starting in Impala 2.2.8, as a result of the changes for IMPALA-2284.
The fix for this issue involves removing a separate memory work area for HBase queries
and reusing other memory that was already allocated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2731](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2731)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title79"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-migration-assignment-of-on-clause-predicates-inside-inline-views-3"></a>

### Fix migration/assignment of On-clause predicates inside inline views

Some combinations of `ON` clauses in join queries could result in comparisons
being applied at the wrong stage of query processing, leading to incorrect results.
Wrong predicate assignment could happen under the following conditions:

- The query includes an inline view that contains an outer join.
- That inline view is joined with another table in the enclosing query block.
- That join has an `ON` clause containing a predicate that
  only references columns originating from the outer-joined tables inside the inline view.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1459](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1459)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title80"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-wrong-predicate-assignment-in-outer-joins"></a>

### Fix wrong predicate assignment in outer joins

The join predicate for an `OUTER JOIN` clause could be applied at the wrong stage
of query processing, leading to incorrect results.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2446](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2446)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title81"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-sending-large-partition-stats-objects-over-thrift-2"></a>

### Avoid sending large partition stats objects over thrift

The catalogd daemon could encounter a serious error when loading the
incremental statistics metadata for tables with large numbers of partitions and columns.
The problem occurred when the internal representation of metadata for the table exceeded 2
GB, for example in a table with 20K partitions and 77 columns. The fix causes a
`COMPUTE INCREMENTAL STATS` operation to fail if it would produce
metadata that exceeded the maximum size.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2648](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2648), [IMPALA-2664](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2664)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title82"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-overflow-when-adding-large-intervals-to-timestamps"></a>

### Avoid overflow when adding large intervals to TIMESTAMPs

Adding or subtracting a large `INTERVAL` value to a
`TIMESTAMP` value could produce an incorrect result, with the value
wrapping instead of returning an out-of-range error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1675](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1675)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title83"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--analysis-exception-when-a-binary-operator-contains-an-in-operator-with-values"></a>

### Analysis exception when a binary operator contains an IN operator with values

An `IN` operator with literal values could cause a statement to fail if used
as the argument to a binary operator, such as an equality test for a `BOOLEAN` value.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1949](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1949)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title84"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--make-max_page_header_size-configurable-2"></a>

### Make MAX\_PAGE\_HEADER\_SIZE configurable

Impala could fail to access Parquet data files with page headers larger than 8 MB, which
could occur, for example, if the minimum or maximum values for a column were long strings.
The fix adds a configuration setting `--max_page_header_size`, which you
can use to increase the Impala size limit to a value higher than 8 MB.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2273](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2273)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title85"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-spilling-sorts-with-var-len-slots-that-are-null-or-empty."></a>

### Fix spilling sorts with var-len slots that are NULL or empty.

A query that activated the spill-to-disk mechanism could fail if it contained a sort expression
involving certain combinations of fixed-length or variable-length types.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2357](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2357)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title86"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--work-around-impala-2344:-fail-query-with-oom-in-case-block-pin-fails"></a>

### Work-around IMPALA-2344: Fail query with OOM in case block->Pin() fails

Some queries that activated the spill-to-disk mechanism could produce a serious error
if there was insufficient memory to set up internal work areas. Now those queries
produce normal out-of-memory errors instead.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2344](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2344)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title87"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--crash-likely-race-tearing-down-bufferedblockmgr-on-query-failure"></a>

### Crash (likely race) tearing down BufferedBlockMgr on query failure

A serious error could occur under rare circumstances, due to a race condition while freeing memory during heavily concurrent workloads.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2252](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2252)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title88"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queryexecstate-doesn-t-check-for-query-cancellation-or-errors"></a>

### QueryExecState doesn't check for query cancellation or errors

A call to `SetError()` in a user-defined function (UDF) would not cause the query to fail as expected.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1746](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1746)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title89"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-throws-illegalstateexception-when-inserting-data-into-a-partition-while-select-subquery-group-by-partition-columns"></a>

### Impala throws IllegalStateException when inserting data into a partition while select subquery group by partition columns

An `INSERT ... SELECT` operation into a partitioned table could fail if the `SELECT` query
included a `GROUP BY` clause referring to the partition key columns.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2533](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2533)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title90"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.8"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.8

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.8.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title91"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-is-unable-to-read-hive-tables-created-with-the-stored-as-avro-clause"></a>

### Impala is unable to read hive tables created with the "STORED AS AVRO" clause

Impala could not read Avro tables created in Hive with the `STORED AS AVRO` clause.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1136](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1136), [IMPALA-2161](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2161)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title92"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--make-parquet-scanner-fail-query-if-the-file-size-metadata-is-stale"></a>

### make Parquet scanner fail query if the file size metadata is stale

If a Parquet file in HDFS was overwritten by a smaller file, Impala could encounter a serious error.
Issuing a `INVALIDATE METADATA` statement before a subsequent query would avoid the error.
The fix allows Impala to handle such inconsistencies in Parquet file length cleanly regardless of whether the
table metadata is up-to-date.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2213](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2213)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title93"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-allocating-stringbuffer-1gb-in-scannercontext::stream::getbytesinternal"></a>

### Avoid allocating StringBuffer > 1GB in ScannerContext::Stream::GetBytesInternal()

Impala could encounter a serious error when reading compressed text files larger than 1 GB. The fix causes Impala
to issue an error message instead in this case.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2249](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2249)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title94"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--disallow-long-1-30-strings-in-group_concat"></a>

### Disallow long (1<<30) strings in group\_concat()

A query using the `group_concat()` function could encounter a serious error if the returned string value was larger than 1 GB.
Now the query fails with an error message in this case.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2284](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2284)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title95"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-fnvhash64to32-with-empty-inputs"></a>

### avoid FnvHash64to32 with empty inputs

An edge case in the algorithm used to distribute data among nodes could result in uneven distribution of work for some queries, with all data sent to the same node.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2270](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2270)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title96"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--the-catalog-does-not-close-the-connection-to-hms-during-table-invalidation"></a>

### The catalog does not close the connection to HMS during table invalidation

A communication error could occur between Impala and the Hive metastore database, causing Impala operations that update
table metadata to fail.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2348](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2348)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title97"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--wrong-dcheck-in-phj::processprobebatch"></a>

### Wrong DCHECK in PHJ::ProcessProbeBatch

Certain queries could encounter a serious error if the spill-to-disk mechanism was activated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2364](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2364)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title98"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-cardinality-0-in-scan-nodes-of-small-tables-and-low-selectivity"></a>

### Avoid cardinality 0 in scan nodes of small tables and low selectivity

Impala could generate a suboptimal query plan for some queries involving small tables.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2165](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2165)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title99"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.7"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.7

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.7.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title100"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--warn-if-table-stats-are-potentially-corrupt."></a>

### Warn if table stats are potentially corrupt.

Impala warns if it detects a discrepancy in table statistics: a table considered to have zero rows even though there are data files present.
In this case, Impala also skips query optimizations that are normally applied to very small tables.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1983:](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1983)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title101"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--pass-correct-child-node-in-2nd-phase-merge-aggregation."></a>

### Pass correct child node in 2nd phase merge aggregation.

A query could encounter a serious error if it included a particular combination of aggregate functions and inline views.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2266](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2266)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title102"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--set-the-output-smap-of-an-emptysetnode-produced-from-an-empty-inline-view."></a>

### Set the output smap of an EmptySetNode produced from an empty inline view.

A query could encounter a serious error if it included an inline view whose subquery had no `FROM` clause.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2216](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2216)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title103"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--set-an-insertstmt-s-result-exprs-from-the-source-statement-s-result-exprs."></a>

### Set an InsertStmt's result exprs from the source statement's result exprs.

A `CREATE TABLE AS SELECT` or `INSERT ... SELECT` statement could produce
different results than a `SELECT` statement, for queries including a `FULL JOIN` clause
and including literal values in the select list.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2203](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2203)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title104"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-planning-of-empty-union-operands-with-analytics."></a>

### Fix planning of empty union operands with analytics.

A query could return incorrect results if it contained a `UNION` clause, calls to analytic functions, and a constant expression that evaluated to `FALSE`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2088](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2088)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title105"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--retain-eq-predicates-bound-by-grouping-slots-with-complex-grouping-exprs."></a>

### Retain eq predicates bound by grouping slots with complex grouping exprs.

A query containing an `INNER JOIN` clause could return undesired rows.
Some predicate specified in the `ON` clause could be omitted from the filtering operation.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2089](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2089)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title106"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--row-count-not-set-for-empty-partition-when-spec-is-used-with-compute-incremental-stats"></a>

### Row count not set for empty partition when spec is used with compute incremental stats

A `COMPUTE INCREMENTAL STATS` statement could leave the row count for an emptyp partition as -1, rather than initializing the row count to 0. The missing statistic value could result in reduced query performance.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2199](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2199)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title107"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--explicit-aliases-ordinals-analysis-bug"></a>

### Explicit aliases + ordinals analysis bug

A query could encounter a serious error if it included column aliases with the same names as table columns, and used
ordinal numbers in an `ORDER BY` or `GROUP BY` clause.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1898](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1898)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title108"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-tupleisnullpredicate-to-return-false-if-no-tuples-are-nullable."></a>

### Fix TupleIsNullPredicate to return false if no tuples are nullable.

A query could return incorrect results if it included an outer join clause, inline views, and calls to functions such as `coalesce()`
that can generate `NULL` values.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1987](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1987)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title109"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-expr::computeresultslayout-logic"></a>

### fix Expr::ComputeResultsLayout() logic

A query could return incorrect results if the table contained multiple `CHAR` columns with length of 2 or less, and the query included a `GROUP BY` clause that referred to multiple such columns.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2178](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2178)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title110"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--substitute-an-insertstmt-s-partition-key-exprs-with-the-root-node-s-smap."></a>

### Substitute an InsertStmt's partition key exprs with the root node's smap.

An `INSERT` statement could encounter a serious error if the `SELECT`
portion called an analytic function.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1737](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1737)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title111"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-impala-2.2.5"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala Impala 2.2.5

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.5.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title112"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-dml-ddl-operations-corrupt-table-metadata-leading-to-hive-query-failures"></a>

### Impala DML/DDL operations corrupt table metadata leading to Hive query failures

When the Impala `COMPUTE STATS` statement was run on a partitioned Parquet table that was created in Hive, the table subsequently became inaccessible in Hive.
The table was still accessible to Impala. Regaining access in Hive required a workaround of creating a new table. The error displayed in Hive was:

```
Error: Error while compiling statement: FAILED: SemanticException Class not found: org.apache.impala.hive.serde.ParquetInputFormat (state=42000,code=40000)
```

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2048](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2048)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title113"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoiding-a-dcheck-of-null-hash-table-in-spilled-right-joins"></a>

### Avoiding a DCHECK of NULL hash table in spilled right joins

A query could encounter a serious error if it contained a `RIGHT OUTER`, `RIGHT ANTI`, or `FULL OUTER` join clause
and approached the memory limit on a host so that the "spill to disk" mechanism was activated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1929](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1929)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title114"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--bug-in-printtcolumnvalue-caused-wrong-stats-for-tinyint-partition-cols"></a>

### Bug in PrintTColumnValue caused wrong stats for TINYINT partition cols

Declaring a partition key column as a `TINYINT` caused problems with the `COMPUTE STATS` statement.
The associated partitions would always have zero estimated rows, leading to potential inefficient query plans.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2136](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2136)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title115"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--where-clause-does-not-propagate-to-joins-inside-nested-views"></a>

### Where clause does not propagate to joins inside nested views

A query that referred to a view whose query referred to another view containing a join, could return incorrect results.
`WHERE` clauses for the outermost query were not always applied, causing the result
set to include additional rows that should have been filtered out.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2018](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2018)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title116"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--add-effective_user-builtin"></a>

### Add effective\_user() builtin

The `user()` function returned the name of the logged-in user, which might not be the
same as the user name being checked for authorization if, for example, delegation was enabled.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2064](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2064)

**Resolution:** Rather than change the behavior of the `user()` function, the fix introduces an additional function `effective_user()` that returns the user name that is checked during authorization.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title117"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--make-utc-to-local-timestampvalue-conversion-faster."></a>

### Make UTC to local TimestampValue conversion faster.

Query performance was improved substantially for Parquet files containing `TIMESTAMP`
data written by Hive, when the `-convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps=true` setting
is in effect.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2125](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2125)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title118"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--workaround-impala-1619-in-bufferedblockmgr::consumememory"></a>

### Workaround IMPALA-1619 in BufferedBlockMgr::ConsumeMemory()

A join query could encounter a serious error if the query
approached the memory limit on a host so that the "spill to disk" mechanism was activated, and data volume in the join was large enough that an internal memory buffer exceeded 1 GB in size on a particular host.
(Exceeding this limit would only happen for huge join queries, because Impala could split this intermediate data
into 16 parts during the join query, and the buffer only contains compact bookkeeping data rather than the actual
join column data.)

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2065](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2065)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title119"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.3"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.3

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.3.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title120"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--enable-using-isilon-as-the-underlying-filesystem."></a>

### Enable using Isilon as the underlying filesystem.

Enabling Impala to work with the Isilon filesystem involves a number of
fixes to performance and flexibility for dealing with I/O using remote reads.
See [Using Impala with Isilon Storage](#impala_isilon--impala_isilon) for details on using Impala and Isilon together.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1968](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1968), [IMPALA-1730](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1730)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title121"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--expand-set-of-supported-timezones."></a>

### Expand set of supported timezones.

The set of timezones recognized by Impala was expanded.
You can always find the latest list of supported timezones in the
Impala source code, in the file
[timezone\_db.cc](https://github.com/apache/impala/blob/master/be/src/exprs/timezone_db.cc).

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1381](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1381)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title122"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-timestamp-iso-8601-support."></a>

### Impala Timestamp ISO-8601 Support.

Impala can now process `TIMESTAMP` literals including a trailing `z`, signifying "Zulu" time, a synonym for UTC.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1963](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1963)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title123"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-wrong-warning-when-insert-overwrite-to-empty-table"></a>

### Fix wrong warning when insert overwrite to empty table

An `INSERT OVERWRITE` operation would encounter an error
if the `SELECT` portion of the statement returned zero
rows, such as with a `LIMIT 0` clause.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2008](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2008)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title124"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--expand-parsing-of-decimals-to-include-scientific-notation"></a>

### Expand parsing of decimals to include scientific notation

`DECIMAL` literals can now include `e` scientific notation.
For example, now `CAST(1e3 AS DECIMAL(5,3))` is a valid expression.
Formerly it returned `NULL`.
Some scientific expressions might have worked before in `DECIMAL` context, but only when the scale was 0.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1952](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1952)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title125"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.1

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.1.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title126"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.2.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.2.0

This section lists the most frequently encountered customer issues fixed in Impala 2.2.0.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.2.0, including over 40 critical issues, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title127"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--altering-a-column-s-type-causes-column-stats-to-stop-sticking-for-that-column"></a>

### Altering a column's type causes column stats to stop sticking for that column

When the type of a column was changed in either Hive or Impala through `ALTER TABLE CHANGE COLUMN`, the metastore database did not correctly propagate
that change to the table that contains the column statistics. The statistics (particularly the `NDV`) for that column were permanently reset
and could not be changed by Impala's `COMPUTE STATS` command. The underlying cause is a Hive bug (HIVE-9866).

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1607](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1607)

**Resolution:** Resolved by incorporating the fix for [HIVE-9866](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HIVE-9866).

**Workaround:** On systems without the corresponding Hive fix, change the column back to its original type. The stats reappear and you can recompute or drop them.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title128"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-may-leak-or-use-too-many-file-descriptors"></a>

### Impala may leak or use too many file descriptors

If a file was truncated in HDFS without a corresponding `REFRESH` in Impala, Impala could allocate memory for file descriptors and not free that memory.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1854](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1854)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title129"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--spurious-stale-block-locality-messages"></a>

### Spurious stale block locality messages

Impala could issue messages stating the block locality metadata was stale, when the metadata was actually fine.
The internal "remote bytes read" counter was not being reset properly.
This issue did not cause an actual slowdown in query execution, but the spurious error could result in unnecessary debugging work
and unnecessary use of the `INVALIDATE METADATA` statement.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1712](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1712)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title130"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--drop-table-fails-after-compute-stats-and-alter-table-rename-to-a-different-database."></a>

### DROP TABLE fails after COMPUTE STATS and ALTER TABLE RENAME to a different database.

When a table was moved from one database to another, the column statistics were not pointed to the new database.i
This could result in lower performance for queries due to unavailable statistics, and also an inability
to drop the table.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1711](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1711)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title131"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-1556-causes-memory-leak-with-secure-connections"></a>

### IMPALA-1556 causes memory leak with secure connections

impalad daemons could experience a memory leak on clusters using Kerberos
authentication, with memory usage growing as more data is transferred across the secure channel, either
to the client program or between Impala nodes. The same issue affected LDAP-secured clusters to a lesser
degree, because the LDAP security only covers data transferred back to client programs.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1674](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1674)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title132"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--unix_timestamp-does-not-return-correct-time"></a>

### unix\_timestamp() does not return correct time

The `unix_timestamp()` function could return an incorrect value (a constant value of 1).

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1623](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1623)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title133"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-incorrectly-handles-text-data-missing-a-newline-on-the-last-line"></a>

### Impala incorrectly handles text data missing a newline on the last line

Some queries did not recognize the final line of a text data file if the line did not end with a newline character.
This could lead to inconsistent results, such as a different number of rows for `SELECT COUNT(*)`
as opposed to `SELECT *`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1476](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1476)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title134"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-s-acls-check-do-not-consider-all-group-acls-only-checked-first-one."></a>

### Impala's ACLs check do not consider all group ACLs, only checked first one.

If the HDFS user ID associated with the impalad process had read or write access in HDFS based on group membership, Impala statements could still fail with HDFS permission errors if that group was not the first listed group for that user ID.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1805](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1805)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title135"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-infinite-loop-opening-or-closing-file-with-invalid-metadata"></a>

### Fix infinite loop opening or closing file with invalid metadata

Truncating a file in HDFS, after Impala had cached the file metadata, could produce a hang when Impala
queried a table containing that file.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1794](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1794)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title136"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--cannot-write-parquet-files-when-values-are-larger-than-64kb"></a>

### Cannot write Parquet files when values are larger than 64KB

Impala could sometimes fail to `INSERT` into a Parquet table if a column value such as a `STRING`
was larger than 64 KB.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1705](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1705)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title137"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-will-not-run-on-certain-intel-cpus"></a>

### Impala Will Not Run on Certain Intel CPUs

This fix relaxes the CPU requirement for Impala. Now only the SSSE3 instruction set is required. Formerly, SSE4.1
instructions were generated, making Impala refuse to start on some older CPUs.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1646](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1646)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title138"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.10"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.10

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title139"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.7"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.7

This section lists the most significant Impala issues fixed in Impala 2.1.7.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title140"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-return-empty-result-if-it-contains-nullliteral-in-inlineview-2"></a>

### Query return empty result if it contains NullLiteral in inlineview

If an inline view in a `FROM` clause contained a `NULL` literal, the result set was empty.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1917](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1917)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title141"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-edge-cases-for-decimal-integer-cast"></a>

### Fix edge cases for decimal/integer cast

A value of type `DECIMAL(3,0)` could be incorrectly cast to `TINYINT`.
The resulting out-of-range value could be incorrect. After the fix, the smallest type that is allowed
for this cast is `INT`, and attempting to use `DECIMAL(3,0)` in a
`TINYINT` context produces a "loss of precision" error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2264](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2264)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title142"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--constant-filter-expressions-are-not-checked-for-errors-and-state-cleanup-on-exception-dcheck-on-destroying-an-exprcontext"></a>

### Constant filter expressions are not checked for errors and state cleanup on exception / DCHECK on destroying an ExprContext

An invalid constant expression in a `WHERE` clause (for example, an invalid
regular expression pattern) could fail to clean up internal state after raising a query error.
Therefore, certain combinations of invalid expressions in a query could cause a crash, or cause a query to continue
when it should halt with an error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1756](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1756), [IMPALA-2514](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2514)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title143"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queryexecstate-does-not-check-for-query-cancellation-or-errors"></a>

### QueryExecState does not check for query cancellation or errors

A call to `SetError()` in a user-defined function (UDF) would not cause the query to fail as expected.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1746](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1746), [IMPALA-2141](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2141)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title144"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.6"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.6

This section lists the most significant Impala issues fixed in Impala 2.1.6.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title145"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--wrong-dcheck-in-phj::processprobebatch-2"></a>

### Wrong DCHECK in PHJ::ProcessProbeBatch

Certain queries could encounter a serious error if the spill-to-disk mechanism was activated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2364](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2364)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title146"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--largestspilledpartition-was-not-checking-if-partition-is-closed"></a>

### LargestSpilledPartition was not checking if partition is closed

Certain queries could encounter a serious error if the spill-to-disk mechanism was activated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2314](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2314)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title147"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-cardinality-0-in-scan-nodes-of-small-tables-and-low-selectivity-2"></a>

### Avoid cardinality 0 in scan nodes of small tables and low selectivity

Impala could generate a suboptimal query plan for some queries involving small tables.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2165](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2165)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title148"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-expr::computeresultslayout-logic-2"></a>

### fix Expr::ComputeResultsLayout() logic

Queries using the `GROUP BY` operator on multiple `CHAR` columns with length less than or equal to 2 characters
could return incorrect results for some columns.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2178](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2178)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title149"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--properly-unescape-string-value-for-hbase-filters"></a>

### Properly unescape string value for HBase filters

Queries against HBase tables could return incomplete results if the `WHERE` clause included string comparisons using literals
containing escaped quotation marks.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-2133](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-2133)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title150"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoiding-a-dcheck-of-null-hash-table-in-spilled-right-joins-2"></a>

### Avoiding a DCHECK of NULL hash table in spilled right joins

A query could encounter a serious error if it contained a `RIGHT OUTER`, `RIGHT ANTI`, or `FULL OUTER` join clause
and approached the memory limit on a host so that the "spill to disk" mechanism was activated.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1929](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1929)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title151"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.5"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.5

This section lists the most significant Impala issues fixed in Impala 2.1.5.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title152"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avoid-calling-processbatch-with-out_batch-atcapacity-in-right-joins"></a>

### Avoid calling ProcessBatch with out\_batch->AtCapacity in right joins

Queries including `RIGHT OUTER JOIN`, `RIGHT ANTI JOIN`, or `FULL OUTER JOIN`
clauses and involving a high volume of data could encounter a serious error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1919](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1919)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title153"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.4"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.4

This section lists the most significant Impala issues fixed in Impala 2.1.4.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title154"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--crash:-impala::tupleisnullpredicate::prepare"></a>

### Crash: impala::TupleIsNullPredicate::Prepare

When expressions that tested for `NULL` were used in combination with analytic functions, an error could occur.
(The original crash issue was fixed by an earlier patch.)

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1519](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1519)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title155"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--expand-parsing-of-decimals-to-include-scientific-notation-2"></a>

### Expand parsing of decimals to include scientific notation

`DECIMAL` literals could include `e` scientific notation.
For example, now `CAST(1e3 AS DECIMAL(5,3))` is a valid expression.
Formerly it returned `NULL`.
Some scientific expressions might have worked before in `DECIMAL` context, but only when the scale was 0.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1952](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1952)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title156"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--insert-ctas-evaluates-and-applies-constant-predicates."></a>

### INSERT/CTAS evaluates and applies constant predicates.

An `INSERT OVERWRITE` statement would write new data, even if
a constant clause such as `WHERE 1 = 0` should have
prevented it from writing any rows.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1860](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1860)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title157"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--assign-predicates-below-analytic-functions-with-a-compatible-partition-by-clause"></a>

### Assign predicates below analytic functions with a compatible partition by clause

If the `PARTITION BY` clause in an analytic function refers to partition key columns in a partitioned table, now Impala can perform partition pruning during the analytic query.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1900](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1900)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title158"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--first_value-may-produce-incorrect-results-with-preceding-windows"></a>

### FIRST\_VALUE may produce incorrect results with preceding windows

A query using the `FIRST_VALUE` analytic function
and a window defined with the `PRECEDING` keyword
could produce wrong results.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1888](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1888)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title159"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--first_value-rewrite-fn-type-might-not-match-slot-type"></a>

### FIRST\_VALUE rewrite fn type might not match slot type

A query referencing a `DECIMAL` column with the `FIRST_VALUE` analytic function
could encounter an error.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1559](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1559)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title160"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--analyticevalnode-cannot-handle-partition-order-by-exprs-with-nan"></a>

### AnalyticEvalNode cannot handle partition/order by exprs with NaN

A query using an analytic function
could encounter an error if the
evaluation of an analytic `ORDER BY` or `PARTITION` expression
resulted in a NaN value, for example if the `ORDER BY` or `PARTITION`
contained a division operation where both operands were zero.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1808](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1808)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title161"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--analyticevalnode-not-properly-handling-nullable-tuples"></a>

### AnalyticEvalNode not properly handling nullable tuples

An analytic function containing only an `OVER` clause could
encounter an error if another part of the query (specifically an outer join)
produced all-`NULL` tuples.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1562](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1562)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title162"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.3"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.3

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.1.3.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title163"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--add-compatibility-flag-for-hive-parquet-timestamps"></a>

### Add compatibility flag for Hive-Parquet-Timestamps

When Hive writes `TIMESTAMP` values, it represents them
in the local time zone of the server. Impala expects `TIMESTAMP`
values to always be in the UTC time zone, possibly leading to inconsistent
results depending on which component created the data files.
This patch introduces a new startup flag, `-convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps`
for the impalad daemon.
Specify `-convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps=true`
to make Impala recognize Parquet data files written by Hive
and automatically adjust `TIMESTAMP`
values read from those files into the UTC time zone for
compatibility with other Impala `TIMESTAMP` processing.
Although this setting is currently turned off by default, consider enabling it if practical in your environment, for maximum interoperability with Hive-created Parquet files.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1658](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1658)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title164"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--use-snprintf-instead-of-lexical_cast-in-float-to-string-casts"></a>

### Use snprintf() instead of lexical\_cast() in float-to-string casts

Converting a floating-point value to a `STRING` could be slower than necessary.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1738](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1738)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title165"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-partition-spilling-cleanup-when-new-stream-ooms"></a>

### Fix partition spilling cleanup when new stream OOMs

Certain calls to aggregate functions with `STRING` arguments could encounter a serious error
when the system ran low on memory and attempted to activate the spill-to-disk mechanism.
The error message referenced the function `impala::AggregateFunctions::StringValGetValue`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1865](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1865)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title166"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-s-acls-check-do-not-consider-all-group-acls-only-checked-first-one.-2"></a>

### Impala's ACLs check do not consider all group ACLs, only checked first one.

If the HDFS user ID associated with the impalad process had read or write access in HDFS based on group membership, Impala statements could still fail with HDFS permission errors if that group was not the first listed group for that user ID.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1805](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1805)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title167"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-infinite-loop-opening-or-closing-file-with-invalid-metadata-2"></a>

### Fix infinite loop opening or closing file with invalid metadata

Truncating a file in HDFS, after Impala had cached the file metadata, could produce a hang when Impala
queried a table containing that file.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1794](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1794)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title168"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--external-data-source-executor-leaking-global-jni-refs"></a>

### external-data-source-executor leaking global jni refs

Successive calls to the data source API could result in excessive memory consumption, with memory allocated but never freed.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1801](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1801)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title169"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--spurious-stale-block-locality-messages-2"></a>

### Spurious stale block locality messages

Impala could issue messages stating the block locality metadata was stale, when the metadata was actually fine.
The internal "remote bytes read" counter was not being reset properly.
This issue did not cause an actual slowdown in query execution, but the spurious error could result in unnecessary debugging work
and unnecessary use of the `INVALIDATE METADATA` statement.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1712](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1712)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title170"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.2

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.1.2.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.1.2, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.1.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title171"></a>
