<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-incorrectly-handles-double-numbers-with-more-than-19-significant-decimal-digits"></a>

### Impala incorrectly handles double numbers with more than 19 significant decimal digits

When a floating-point value was read from a text file and interpreted as a `FLOAT`
or `DOUBLE` value, it could be incorrectly interpreted if it included more than
19 significant digits.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1622](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1622)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title172"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--unix_timestamp-does-not-return-correct-time-2"></a>

### unix\_timestamp() does not return correct time

The `unix_timestamp()` function could return an incorrect value (a constant value of 1).

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1623](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1623)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title173"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--row-count-mismatch:-partition-pruning-with-null"></a>

### Row Count Mismatch: Partition pruning with NULL

A query against a partitioned table could return incorrect results if the `WHERE` clause
compared the partition key to `NULL` using operators such as `=` or `!=`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1535](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1535)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title174"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fetch-column-stats-in-bulk-using-new-hive-.13-hms-apis"></a>

### Fetch column stats in bulk using new (Hive .13) HMS APIs

The performance of the `COMPUTE STATS` statement and queries was improved, particularly for wide tables.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1120](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1120)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title175"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.1

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.1.1.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.1.1, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.1.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title176"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-1556-causes-memory-leak-with-secure-connections-2"></a>

### IMPALA-1556 causes memory leak with secure connections

impalad daemons could experience a memory leak on clusters using Kerberos
authentication, with memory usage growing as more data is transferred across the secure channel, either
to the client program or between Impala nodes. The same issue affected LDAP-secured clusters to a lesser
degree, because the LDAP security only covers data transferred back to client programs.

**Bug:**
<https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1674> IMPALA-1674

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title177"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--tsaslservertransport::factory::gettransport-leaks-transport-map-entries"></a>

### TSaslServerTransport::Factory::getTransport() leaks transport map entries

impalad daemons in clusters secured by Kerberos or LDAP could experience a slight
memory leak on each connection. The accumulation of unreleased memory could cause problems on
long-running clusters.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1668](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1668)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title178"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.1.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.1.0

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.1.0.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.1.0, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title179"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--kerberos-fetches-3x-slower"></a>

### Kerberos fetches 3x slower

Transferring large result sets back to the client application on Kerberos

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1455](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1455)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title180"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--compressed-file-needs-to-be-hold-on-entirely-in-memory"></a>

### Compressed file needs to be hold on entirely in Memory

Queries on gzipped text files required holding the entire data file and its uncompressed representation
in memory at the same time. `SELECT` and `COMPUTE STATS` statements could
fail or perform inefficiently as a result. The fix enables streaming reads for gzipped text, so that the
data is uncompressed as it is read.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1556](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1556)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title181"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--cannot-read-hbase-metadata-with-nullpointerexception:-null"></a>

### Cannot read hbase metadata with NullPointerException: null

Impala might not be able to access HBase tables, depending on the associated levels of Impala and HBase
on the system.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1611](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1611)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title182"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--serious-errors-crashes"></a>

### Serious errors / crashes

Improved code coverage in Impala testing uncovered a number of potentially serious errors that could
occur with specific query syntax. These errors are resolved in Impala 2.1.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1553](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1553), [IMPALA-1528](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1528), [IMPALA-1526](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1526), [IMPALA-1524](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1524), [IMPALA-1508](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1508), [IMPALA-1493](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1493), [IMPALA-1501](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1501), [IMPALA-1483](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1483)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title183"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.5"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.5

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.5, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0.5%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title184"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.4"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.4

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.0.4.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.4, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0.4%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title185"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--add-compatibility-flag-for-hive-parquet-timestamps-2"></a>

### Add compatibility flag for Hive-Parquet-Timestamps

When Hive writes `TIMESTAMP` values, it represents them
in the local time zone of the server. Impala expects `TIMESTAMP`
values to always be in the UTC time zone, possibly leading to inconsistent
results depending on which component created the data files.
This patch introduces a new startup flag, `-convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps`
for the impalad daemon.
Specify `-convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps=true`
to make Impala recognize Parquet data files written by Hive
and automatically adjust `TIMESTAMP`
values read from those files into the UTC time zone for
compatibility with other Impala `TIMESTAMP` processing.
Although this setting is currently turned off by default, consider enabling it if practical in your environment, for maximum interoperability with Hive-created Parquet files.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1658](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1658)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title186"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--iomgr-infinite-loop-opening-closing-file-when-shorter-than-cached-metadata-size"></a>

### IoMgr infinite loop opening/closing file when shorter than cached metadata size

If a table data file was replaced by a shorter file outside of Impala, such as with `INSERT OVERWRITE` in Hive producing an empty
output file, subsequent Impala queries could hang.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1794](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1794)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title187"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.3"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.3

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.0.3.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.3, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0.3%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title188"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--anti-join-could-produce-incorrect-results-when-spilling"></a>

### Anti join could produce incorrect results when spilling

An anti-join query (or a `NOT EXISTS` operation that was rewritten internally into an anti-join) could produce incorrect results
if Impala reached its memory limit, causing the query to write temporary results to disk.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1471](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1471)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title189"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--row-count-mismatch:-partition-pruning-with-null-2"></a>

### Row Count Mismatch: Partition pruning with NULL

A query against a partitioned table could return incorrect results if the `WHERE` clause
compared the partition key to `NULL` using operators such as `=` or `!=`.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1535](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1535)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title190"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fetch-column-stats-in-bulk-using-new-hive-.13-hms-apis-2"></a>

### Fetch column stats in bulk using new (Hive .13) HMS APIs

The performance of the `COMPUTE STATS` statement and queries was improved, particularly for wide tables.

**Bug:** [IMPALA-1120](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1120)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title191"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.2

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.0.2.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.2, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title192"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--group-by-on-string-column-produces-inconsistent-results"></a>

### GROUP BY on STRING column produces inconsistent results

Some operations in queries submitted through Hue or other HiveServer2 clients could produce inconsistent
results.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1453](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1453)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title193"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fix-leaked-file-descriptor-and-excessive-file-descriptor-use"></a>

### Fix leaked file descriptor and excessive file descriptor use

Impala could encounter an error from running out of file descriptors. The fix reduces the amount of time
file descriptors are kept open, and avoids leaking file descriptors when read operations encounter
errors.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title194"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--unix_timestamp-does-not-return-correct-time-3"></a>

### unix\_timestamp() does not return correct time

The `unix_timestamp()` function could return a constant value `1` instead
of a representation of the time.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1623](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1623)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title195"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-should-randomly-select-cached-replica"></a>

### Impala should randomly select cached replica

To avoid putting too heavy a load on any one node, Impala now randomizes which scan node processes each
HDFS data block rather than choosing the first cached block replica.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1586](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1586)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title196"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-always-give-short-name-to-llama."></a>

### Impala does not always give short name to Llama.

In clusters secured by Kerberos or LDAP, a discrepancy in internal transmission of user names could cause
a communication error with Llama.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1606](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1606)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title197"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--accept-unmangled-native-udf-symbols"></a>

### accept unmangled native UDF symbols

The `CREATE FUNCTION` statement could report that it could not find a function entry point
within the `.so` file for a UDF written in C++, even if the corresponding function was
present.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1475](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1475)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title198"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.1

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.0.1.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.1, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title199"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queries-fail-with-metastore-exception-after-upgrade-and-compute-stats"></a>

### Queries fail with metastore exception after upgrade and compute stats

After running the `COMPUTE STATS` statement on an Impala table, subsequent queries on that
table could fail with the exception message `Failed to load metadata for table:
default.stats_test`.

**Bug:**
<https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1416> IMPALA-1416

**Workaround:** Upgrading to a level of  that includes the fix for HIVE-8627, prevents the problem from affecting future `COMPUTE STATS` statements. On affected levels
of , or for Impala tables that have become inaccessible, the workaround is to disable the
`hive.metastore.try.direct.sql` setting in the Hive metastore
hive-site.xml file and issue the `INVALIDATE METADATA` statement for
the affected table. You do not need to rerun the `COMPUTE STATS` statement for the table.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title200"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-2.0.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 2.0.0

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 2.0.0.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 2.0.0, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+2.0%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title201"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--join-hint-is-dropped-when-used-inside-a-view"></a>

### Join Hint is dropped when used inside a view

Hints specified within a view query did not take effect when the view was queried, leading to slow
performance. As part of this fix, Impala now supports hints embedded within comments.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-995"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-995)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title202"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--where-condition-ignored-in-simple-query-with-right-join"></a>

### WHERE condition ignored in simple query with RIGHT JOIN

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1101"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1101)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title203"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-with-self-joined-table-may-produce-incorrect-results"></a>

### Query with self joined table may produce incorrect results

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1102"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1102)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title204"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--incorrect-plan-after-reordering-predicates-inner-join-following-outer-join"></a>

### Incorrect plan after reordering predicates (inner join following outer join)

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1118"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1118)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title205"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--combining-fragments-with-compatible-data-partitions-can-lead-to-incorrect-results-due-to-type-incompatibilities-missing-casts-."></a>

### Combining fragments with compatible data partitions can lead to incorrect results due to type incompatibilities (missing casts).

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1123"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1123)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title206"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--predicate-dropped:-inline-view-distinct-aggregate-in-outer-query"></a>

### Predicate dropped: Inline view + DISTINCT aggregate in outer query

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1165"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1165)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title207"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--reuse-of-a-column-in-join-predicate-may-lead-to-incorrect-results"></a>

### Reuse of a column in JOIN predicate may lead to incorrect results

Potential wrong results for some types of queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1353"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1353)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title208"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--usage-of-trunc-with-string-timestamp-reliably-crashes-node"></a>

### Usage of TRUNC with string timestamp reliably crashes node

Serious error for certain combinations of function calls and data types.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1105"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1105)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title209"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--timestamp-cast-returns-invalid-timestamp"></a>

### Timestamp Cast Returns invalid TIMESTAMP

Serious error for certain combinations of function calls and data types.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1109"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1109)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title210"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--illegalstateexception-upon-join-of-decimal-columns-with-different-precision"></a>

### IllegalStateException upon JOIN of DECIMAL columns with different precision

`DECIMAL` columns with different precision could not be compared in join predicates.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1121"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1121)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title211"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--allow-creating-avro-tables-without-column-definitions.-allow-compute-stats-to-always-work-on-impala-created-avro-tables."></a>

### Allow creating Avro tables without column definitions. Allow COMPUTE STATS to always work on Impala-created Avro tables.

Hive-created Avro tables with columns specified by a JSON file or literal could produce errors when
queried in Impala, and could not be used with the `COMPUTE STATS` statement. Now you can
create such tables in Impala to avoid such errors.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1104"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1104)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title212"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ensure-all-webserver-output-is-escaped"></a>

### Ensure all webserver output is escaped

The Impala debug web UI did not properly encode all output.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1133"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1133)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title213"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queries-with-union-in-inline-view-have-empty-resource-requests"></a>

### Queries with union in inline view have empty resource requests

Certain queries could run without obeying the limits imposed by resource management.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1236"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1236)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title214"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-employ-acls-when-checking-path-permissions-for-load-and-insert"></a>

### Impala does not employ ACLs when checking path permissions for LOAD and INSERT

Certain `INSERT` and `LOAD DATA` statements could fail unnecessarily, if
the target directories in HDFS had restrictive HDFS permissions, but those permissions were overridden by
HDFS extended ACLs.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1279"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1279)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title215"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-map-principals-to-lowercase-affecting-sentry-authorisation"></a>

### Impala does not map principals to lowercase, affecting Sentry authorisation

In a Kerberos environment, the principal name was not mapped to lowercase, causing issues when a user
logged in with an uppercase principal name and Sentry authorization was enabled.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1334"](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1334)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title216"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.4.4"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.4.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title217"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.4.3"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.4.3

Impala 1.4.3 includes fixes to address what is known as the POODLE vulnerability in SSLv3. SSLv3 access is
disabled in the Impala debug web UI.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title218"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.4.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.4.2

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.4.2.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 1.4.2, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.4.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title219"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.4.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.4.1

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.4.1.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 1.4.1, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.4.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title220"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-terminating-with-boost-exception"></a>

### impalad terminating with Boost exception

Occasionally, a non-trivial query run through Llama could encounter a serious error. The detailed error
in the log was:

```
boost::exception_detail::clone_impl
  <boost::exception_detail::error_info_injector<boost::lock_error> >
```

**Severity:** High

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title221"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-uses-wrong-string-format-when-writing-logs"></a>

### Impalad uses wrong string format when writing logs

Impala log files could contain internal error messages due to a problem formatting certain strings. The
messages consisted of a Java call stack starting with:

```
jni-util.cc:177] java.util.MissingFormatArgumentException: Format specifier 's'
```

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title222"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--update-hs2-client-api."></a>

### Update HS2 client API.

A downlevel version of the HiveServer2 API could cause difficulty retrieving the precision and scale of a
`DECIMAL` value.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1107](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1107)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title223"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-catalog-updates-can-fail-with-error:-illegalargumentexception:-fromkey-out-of-range"></a>

### Impalad catalog updates can fail with error: "IllegalArgumentException: fromKey out of range"

The error in the title could occur following a DDL statement. This issue was discovered during internal
testing and has not been reported in customer environments.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1093](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1093)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title224"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--total-time-counter-does-not-capture-all-the-network-transmit-time"></a>

### "Total" time counter does not capture all the network transmit time

The time for some network operations was not counted in the report of total time for a query, making it
difficult to diagnose network-related performance issues.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1131](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1131)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title225"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-will-crash-when-reading-certain-avro-files-containing-bytes-data"></a>

### Impala will crash when reading certain Avro files containing bytes data

Certain Avro fields for byte data could cause Impala to be unable to read an Avro data file, even if the
field was not part of the Impala table definition. With this fix, Impala can now read these Avro data
files, although Impala queries cannot refer to the "bytes" fields.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1149](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1149)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title226"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--support-specifying-a-custom-authorizationprovider-in-impala"></a>

### Support specifying a custom AuthorizationProvider in Impala

The `--authorization_policy_provider_class` option for impalad was
added back. This option specifies a custom `AuthorizationProvider` class rather than the
default `HadoopGroupAuthorizationProvider`. It had been used for internal testing, then
removed in Impala 1.4.0, but it was considered useful by some customers.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1142](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1142)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title227"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.4.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.4.0

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.4.0.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 1.4.0, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.4%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title228"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--failed-dcheck-in-disk-io-mgr-reader-context.cc:174"></a>

### Failed DCHECK in disk-io-mgr-reader-context.cc:174

The serious error in the title could occur, with the supplemental message:

```
num_used_buffers_ < 0: #used=-1 during cancellation HDFS cached data
```

The issue was due to the use of HDFS caching with data files accessed by Impala. Support for HDFS caching
in Impala was introduced in Impala 1.4.0. The fix for this issue was backported to Impala
1.3.x, and is the only change in Impala 1.3.2.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1019](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1019)

**Resolution:** This issue is fixed in Impala 1.3.2. The addition of HDFS caching
support in Impala 1.4 means that this issue does not apply to any new level of Impala.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title229"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-shell-only-works-with-ascii-characters"></a>

### impala-shell only works with ASCII characters

The impala-shell interpreter could encounter errors processing SQL statements
containing non-ASCII characters.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-489](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-489)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title230"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--the-extended-view-definition-sql-text-in-views-created-by-impala-should-always-have-fully-qualified-table-names"></a>

### The extended view definition SQL text in Views created by Impala should always have fully-qualified table names

When a view was accessed while inside a different database, references to tables were not resolved unless
the names were fully qualified when the view was created.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-962](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-962)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title231"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-forgets-about-partitions-with-non-existant-locations"></a>

### Impala forgets about partitions with non-existant locations

If an `ALTER TABLE` specified a non-existent HDFS location for a partition, afterwards
Impala would not be able to access the partition at all.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-741](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-741)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title232"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--create-table-like-fails-if-source-is-a-view"></a>

### CREATE TABLE LIKE fails if source is a view

The `CREATE TABLE LIKE` clause was enhanced to be able to create a table with the same
column definitions as a view. The resulting table is a text table unless the `STORED AS`
clause is specified, because a view does not have an associated file format to inherit.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-834](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-834)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title233"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--improve-partition-pruning-time"></a>

### Improve partition pruning time

Operations on tables with many partitions could be slow due to the time to evaluate which partitions were
affected. The partition pruning code was speeded up substantially.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-887](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-887)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title234"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--improve-compute-stats-performance"></a>

### Improve compute stats performance

The performance of the `COMPUTE STATS` statement was improved substantially. The
efficiency of its internal operations was improved, and some statistics are no longer gathered because
they are not currently used for planning Impala queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1003](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1003)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title235"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--when-i-run-create-table-new_table-like-avro_table-the-schema-does-not-get-mapped-properly-from-an-avro-schema-to-a-hive-schema"></a>

### When I run CREATE TABLE new\_table LIKE avro\_table, the schema does not get mapped properly from an avro schema to a hive schema

After a `CREATE TABLE LIKE` statement using an Avro table as the source, the new table
could have incorrect metadata and be inaccessible, depending on how the original Avro table was created.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-185](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-185)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title236"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--race-condition-in-iomgr.-blocked-ranges-enqueued-after-cancel."></a>

### Race condition in IoMgr. Blocked ranges enqueued after cancel.

Impala could encounter a serious error after a query was cancelled.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1046](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1046)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title237"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--deadlock-in-scan-node"></a>

### Deadlock in scan node

A deadlock condition could make all impalad daemons hang, making the cluster
unresponsive for Impala queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1083](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1083)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title238"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.3.3"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.3.3

Impala 1.3.3 includes fixes to address what is known as the POODLE vulnerability in SSLv3. SSLv3 access is
disabled in the Impala debug web UI.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title239"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.3.2"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.3.2

This backported bug fix is the only change between Impala 1.3.1 and Impala 1.3.2.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title240"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--failed-dcheck-in-disk-io-mgr-reader-context.cc:174-2"></a>

### Failed DCHECK in disk-io-mgr-reader-context.cc:174

The serious error in the title could occur, with the supplemental message:

```
num_used_buffers_ < 0: #used=-1 during cancellation HDFS cached data
```

The issue was due to the use of HDFS caching with data files accessed by Impala. Support for HDFS caching
in Impala was introduced in Impala 1.4.0. The fix for this issue was backported to Impala
1.3.x, and is the only change in Impala 1.3.2.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-1019](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-1019)

**Resolution:** This issue is fixed in Impala 1.3.2. The addition of HDFS caching
support in Impala 1.4 means that this issue does not apply to any new level of Impala.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title241"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.3.1"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.3.1

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.3.1.

For the full list of fixed issues in Impala 1.3.1, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.3.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title242"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-crashes-when-left-joining-inline-view-that-has-aggregate-using-distinct"></a>

### Impalad crashes when left joining inline view that has aggregate using distinct

Impala could encounter a severe error in a query combining a left outer join with an inline view
containing a `COUNT(DISTINCT)` operation.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-904](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-904)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title243"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--incorrect-result-with-group-by-query-with-null-value-in-group-by-data"></a>

### Incorrect result with group by query with null value in group by data

If the result of a `GROUP BY` operation is `NULL`, the resulting row might
be omitted from the result set. This issue depends on the data values and data types in the table.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-901](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-901)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title244"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--drop-function-does-not-clear-local-library-cache"></a>

### Drop Function does not clear local library cache

When a UDF is dropped through the `DROP FUNCTION` statement, and then the UDF is
re-created with a new `.so` library or JAR file, the original version of the UDF is still
used when the UDF is called from queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-786](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-786)

**Workaround:** Restart the impalad daemon on all nodes.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title245"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--compute-stats-doesn-t-propagate-underlying-error-correctly"></a>

### Compute stats doesn't propagate underlying error correctly

If a `COMPUTE STATS` statement encountered an error, the error message is "Query
aborted" with no further detail. Common reasons why a `COMPUTE STATS` statement might
fail include network errors causing the coordinator node to lose contact with other
impalad instances, and column names that match Impala
[reserved words](#impala_reserved_words--reserved_words). (Currently, if a column name
is an Impala reserved word, `COMPUTE STATS` always returns an error.)

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-762](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-762)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title246"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--inserts-should-respect-changes-in-partition-location"></a>

### Inserts should respect changes in partition location

After an `ALTER TABLE` statement that changes the `LOCATION` property of a
partition, a subsequent `INSERT` statement would always use a path derived from the base
data directory for the table.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-624](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-624)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title247"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--text-data-with-carriage-returns-generates-wrong-results-for-count"></a>

### Text data with carriage returns generates wrong results for count(\*)

A `COUNT(*)` operation could return the wrong result for text tables using nul characters
(ASCII value 0) as delimiters.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-13](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-13)

**Workaround:** Impala adds support for ASCII 0 characters as delimiters through the clause
`FIELDS TERMINATED BY '\0'`.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title248"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--io-mgr-should-take-instance-memory-limit-into-account-when-creating-io-buffers"></a>

### IO Mgr should take instance memory limit into account when creating io buffers

Impala could allocate more memory than necessary during certain operations.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-488](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-488)

**Workaround:** Before issuing a `COMPUTE STATS` statement for a Parquet table, reduce
the number of threads used in that operation by issuing `SET NUM_SCANNER_THREADS=2` in
impala-shell. Then issue `UNSET NUM_SCANNER_THREADS` before continuing
with queries.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title249"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-should-provide-an-option-for-new-sub-directories-to-automatically-inherit-the-permissions-of-the-parent-directory"></a>

### Impala should provide an option for new sub directories to automatically inherit the permissions of the parent directory

When new subdirectories are created underneath a partitioned table by an `INSERT`
statement, previously the new subdirectories always used the default HDFS permissions for the
`impala` user, which might not be suitable for directories intended to be read and written
by other components also.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-827](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-827)

**Resolution:** In Impala 1.3.1 and higher, you can specify the
`--insert_inherit_permissions` configuration when starting the impalad
daemon.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title250"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--illegal-state-exception-or-crash-in-query-with-union-in-inline-view"></a>

### Illegal state exception (or crash) in query with UNION in inline view

Impala could encounter a severe error in a query where the `FROM` list contains an inline
view that includes a `UNION`. The exact type of the error varies.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-888](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-888)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title251"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--insert-column-reordering-doesn-t-work-with-select-clause"></a>

### INSERT column reordering doesn't work with SELECT clause

The ability to specify a subset of columns in an `INSERT` statement, with order different
than in the target table, was not working as intended.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-945](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-945)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title252"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-impala-1.3.0"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Impala 1.3.0

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.3.0, primarily issues that could cause
wrong results, or cause problems running the `COMPUTE STATS` statement, which is very
important for performance and scalability.

For the full list of fixed issues, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.3%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title253"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--inner-join-after-right-join-may-produce-wrong-results"></a>

### Inner join after right join may produce wrong results

The automatic join reordering optimization could incorrectly reorder queries with an outer join or semi
join followed by an inner join, producing incorrect results.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-860](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-860)

**Workaround:** Including the `STRAIGHT_JOIN` keyword in the query prevented the issue
from occurring.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title254"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--incorrect-results-with-codegen-on-multi-column-group-by-with-nulls."></a>

### Incorrect results with codegen on multi-column group by with NULLs.

A query with a `GROUP BY` clause referencing multiple columns could introduce incorrect
`NULL` values in some columns of the result set. The incorrect `NULL`
values could appear in rows where a different `GROUP BY` column actually did return
`NULL`.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-850](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-850)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title255"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--using-distinct-inside-aggregate-function-may-cause-incorrect-result-when-using-having-clause"></a>

### Using distinct inside aggregate function may cause incorrect result when using having clause

A query could return incorrect results if it combined an aggregate function call, a
`DISTINCT` operator, and a `HAVING` clause, without a `GROUP
BY` clause.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-845](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-845)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title256"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--aggregation-on-union-inside-inline-view-not-distributed-properly."></a>

### Aggregation on union inside (inline) view not distributed properly.

An aggregation query or a query with `ORDER BY` and `LIMIT` could be
executed on a single node in some cases, rather than distributed across the cluster. This issue affected
queries whose `FROM` clause referenced an inline view containing a `UNION`.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-831](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-831)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title257"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--wrong-expression-may-be-used-in-aggregate-query-if-there-are-multiple-similar-expressions"></a>

### Wrong expression may be used in aggregate query if there are multiple similar expressions

If a `GROUP BY` query referenced the same columns multiple times using different
operators, result rows could contain multiple copies of the same expression.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-817](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-817)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title258"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--incorrect-results-when-changing-the-order-of-aggregates-in-the-select-list-with-codegen-enabled"></a>

### Incorrect results when changing the order of aggregates in the select list with codegen enabled

Referencing the same columns in both a `COUNT()` and a `SUM()` call in the
same query, or some other combinations of aggregate function calls, could incorrectly return a result of
0 from one of the aggregate functions. This issue affected references to `TINYINT` and
`SMALLINT` columns, but not `INT` or `BIGINT` columns.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-765](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-765)

**Workaround:** Setting the query option `DISABLE_CODEGEN=TRUE` prevented the incorrect
results. Switching the order of the function calls could also prevent the issue from occurring.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title259"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--union-queries-give-wrong-result-in-a-union-followed-by-sigsegv-in-another-union"></a>

### Union queries give Wrong result in a UNION followed by SIGSEGV in another union

A `UNION` query could produce a wrong result, followed by a serious error for a subsequent
`UNION` query.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-723](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-723)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title260"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--string-data-in-mr-produced-parquet-files-may-be-read-incorrectly"></a>

### String data in MR-produced parquet files may be read incorrectly

Impala could return incorrect string results when reading uncompressed Parquet data files containing
multiple row groups. This issue only affected Parquet data files produced by MapReduce jobs.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-729](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-729)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title261"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--compute-stats-need-to-use-quotes-with-identifiers-that-are-impala-keywords"></a>

### Compute stats need to use quotes with identifiers that are Impala keywords

Using a column or table name that conflicted with Impala keywords could prevent running the
`COMPUTE STATS` statement for the table.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-777](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-777)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title262"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--compute-stats-child-queries-do-not-inherit-parent-query-options."></a>

### COMPUTE STATS child queries do not inherit parent query options.

The `COMPUTE STATS` statement did not use the setting of the `MEM_LIMIT`
query option in impala-shell, potentially causing problems gathering statistics for
wide Parquet tables.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-903](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-903)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title263"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--compute-stats-should-update-partitions-in-batches"></a>

### COMPUTE STATS should update partitions in batches

The `COMPUTE STATS` statement could be slow or encounter a timeout while analyzing a table
with many partitions.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-880](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-880)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title264"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--fail-early-in-analysis-when-compute-stats-is-run-against-avro-table-with-no-columns"></a>

### Fail early (in analysis) when COMPUTE STATS is run against Avro table with no columns

If the columns for an Avro table were all defined in the `TBLPROPERTIES` or
`SERDEPROPERTIES` clauses, the `COMPUTE STATS` statement would fail after
completely analyzing the table, potentially causing a long delay. Although the `COMPUTE
STATS` statement still does not work for such tables, now the problem is detected and reported
immediately.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-867](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-867)

**Workaround:** Re-create the Avro table with columns defined in SQL style, using the output of
`SHOW CREATE TABLE`. (See the JIRA page for detailed steps.)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title265"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.2.4-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.2.4 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.2.4. For the full list of fixed issues, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.2.4%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title266"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--the-catalog-server-exits-with-an-oom-error-after-a-certain-number-of-create-statements"></a>

### The Catalog Server exits with an OOM error after a certain number of CREATE statements

A large number of concurrent `CREATE TABLE` statements can cause the
catalogd process to consume excessive memory, and potentially be killed due to an
out-of-memory condition.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-818](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-818)

**Workaround:** Restart the catalogd service and re-try the DDL operations that
failed.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title267"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--catalog-server-consumes-excessive-cpu-cycle"></a>

### Catalog Server consumes excessive cpu cycle

A large number of tables and partitions could result in unnecessary CPU overhead during Impala idle time
and background operations.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-821](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-821)

**Resolution:** Catalog server processing was optimized in several ways.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title268"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-against-avro-table-crashes-impala-with-codegen-enabled"></a>

### Query against Avro table crashes Impala with codegen enabled

A query against a `TIMESTAMP` column in an Avro table could encounter a serious issue.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-828](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-828)

**Workaround:** Set the query option `DISABLE_CODEGEN=TRUE`

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title269"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--statestore-seems-to-send-concurrent-heartbeats-to-the-same-subscriber-leading-to-repeated-subscriber-hostname-is-registering-with-statestore-ignoring-update-messages"></a>

### Statestore seems to send concurrent heartbeats to the same subscriber leading to repeated "Subscriber 'hostname' is registering with statestore, ignoring update" messages

Impala nodes could produce repeated error messages after recovering from a communication error with the
statestore service.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-809](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-809)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title270"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--join-predicate-incorrectly-ignored"></a>

### Join predicate incorrectly ignored

A join query could produce wrong results if multiple equality comparisons between the same tables
referred to the same column.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-805](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-805)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title271"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-result-differing-between-impala-and-hive"></a>

### Query result differing between Impala and Hive

Certain outer join queries could return wrong results. If one of the tables involved in the join was an
inline view, some tests from the `WHERE` clauses could be applied to the wrong phase of
the query.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title272"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--arrayindexoutofboundsexception-invalid-query-handle-when-reading-large-hbase-cell"></a>

### ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException / Invalid query handle when reading large HBase cell

An HBase cell could contain a value larger than 32 KB, leading to a serious error when Impala queries
that table. The error could occur even if the applicable row is not part of the result set.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-715](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-715)

**Workaround:** Use smaller values in the HBase table, or exclude the column containing the large
value from the result set.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title273"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--select-with-distinct-and-full-outer-join-impalad-coredump"></a>

### select with distinct and full outer join, impalad coredump

A query involving a `DISTINCT` operator combined with a `FULL OUTER JOIN`
could encounter a serious error.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-735](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-735)

**Workaround:** Set the query option `DISABLE_CODEGEN=TRUE`

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title274"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-cannot-load-tables-with-more-than-short.max_value-number-of-partitions"></a>

### Impala cannot load tables with more than Short.MAX\_VALUE number of partitions

If a table had more than 32,767 partitions, Impala would not recognize the partitions above the 32K limit
and query results could be incomplete.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-749](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-749)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title275"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--various-issues-with-hbase-row-key-specification"></a>

### Various issues with HBase row key specification

Queries against HBase tables could fail with an error if the row key was compared to a function return
value rather than a string constant. Also, queries against HBase tables could fail if the
`WHERE` clause contained combinations of comparisons that could not possibly match any row
key.

**Resolution:** Queries now return appropriate results when function calls are used in the row key
comparison. For queries involving non-existent row keys, such as `WHERE row_key
IS NULL` or where the lower bound is greater than the upper bound, the query succeeds and returns
an empty result set.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title276"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.2.3-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.2.3 Release

This release is a fix release that supercedes Impala 1.2.2, with the same features and fixes as 1.2.2 plus
one additional fix for compatibility with Parquet files generated outside of Impala by components such as
Hive, Pig, or MapReduce.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title277"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-cannot-read-parquet-files-with-multiple-row-groups"></a>

### Impala cannot read Parquet files with multiple row groups

An early version of the `parquet-mr` library writes files that are not readable by
Impala, due to the presence of multiple row groups. Queries involving these data files might result in a
crash or a failure with an error such as "Column chunk should not contain two dictionary pages".

This issue does not occur for Parquet files produced by Impala `INSERT` statements, because Impala only produces files with a single row group.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-720](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-720)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title278"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.2.2-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.2.2 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.2.2. For the full list of fixed issues, see [this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.2.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title279"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--order-of-table-references-in-from-clause-is-critical-for-optimal-performance"></a>

### Order of table references in FROM clause is critical for optimal performance

Impala does not currently optimize the join order of queries; instead, it joins tables in the order in
which they are listed in the FROM clause. Queries that contain one or more large tables on the right hand
side of joins (either an explicit join expressed as a JOIN statement or a join implicit in the list of
table references in the FROM clause) may run slowly or crash Impala due to out-of-memory errors. For
example:

```
SELECT ... FROM small_table JOIN large_table
```

**Anticipated Resolution**: Fixed in Impala 1.2.2.

**Workaround**: In Impala 1.2.2 and higher, use the `COMPUTE STATS` statement to gather
statistics for each table involved in the join query, after data is loaded. Prior to Impala 1.2.2, modify
the query, if possible, to join the largest table first. For example:

```
SELECT ... FROM small_table JOIN large_table
```

should be modified to:

```
SELECT ... FROM large_table JOIN small_table
```

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title280"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--parquet-in-cdh4.5-writes-data-files-that-are-sometimes-unreadable-by-impala"></a>

### Parquet in CDH4.5 writes data files that are sometimes unreadable by Impala

Some Parquet files could be generated by other components that Impala could not read.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-694](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-694)

**Resolution:** The underlying issue is being addressed by a fix in the Parquet libraries. Impala
1.2.2 works around the problem and reads the existing data files.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title281"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--deadlock-in-statestore-when-unregistering-a-subscriber-and-building-a-topic-update"></a>

### Deadlock in statestore when unregistering a subscriber and building a topic update

The statestore service cound experience an internal error leading to a hang.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-699](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-699)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title282"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--illegalstateexception-when-doing-a-union-involving-a-group-by"></a>

### IllegalStateException when doing a union involving a group by

A `UNION` query where one side involved a `GROUP BY` operation could cause
a serious error.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-687](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-687)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title283"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-parquet-writer-hit-dcheck-in-rleencoder"></a>

### Impala Parquet Writer hit DCHECK in RleEncoder

A serious error could occur when doing an `INSERT` into a Parquet table.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-689](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-689)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title284"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hive-udf-jars-cannot-be-loaded-by-the-fe"></a>

### Hive UDF jars cannot be loaded by the FE

If the JAR file for a Java-based Hive UDF was not in the `CLASSPATH`, the UDF could not be
called during a query.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-695](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-695)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title285"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.2.1-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.2.1 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.2.1. For the full list of fixed issues, see [this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.2.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title286"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--scanners-use-too-much-memory-when-reading-past-scan-range"></a>

### Scanners use too much memory when reading past scan range

While querying a table with long column values, Impala could over-allocate memory leading to an
out-of-memory error. This problem was observed most frequently with tables using uncompressed RCFile or
text data files.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-525](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-525)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 1.2.1

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title287"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--join-node-consumes-memory-way-beyond-mem-limit"></a>

### Join node consumes memory way beyond mem-limit

A join query could allocate a temporary work area that was larger than needed, leading to an
out-of-memory error. The fix makes Impala return unused memory to the system when the memory limit is
reached, avoiding unnecessary memory errors.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-657](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-657)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 1.2.1

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title288"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--excessive-memory-consumption-when-query-tables-with-1k-columns-parquet-file"></a>

### Excessive memory consumption when query tables with 1k columns (Parquet file)

Impala could encounter an out-of-memory condition setting up work areas for Parquet tables with many
columns. The fix reduces the size of the allocated memory when not actually needed to hold table data.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-652](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-652)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 1.2.1

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title289"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.2.0-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.2.0 Beta Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.2 (beta). For the full list of fixed
issues, see [this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.2%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title290"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.1.1-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.1.1 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.1.1. For the full list of fixed issues, see [this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.1.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title291"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--unexpected-llvm-crash-when-querying-doubles-on-centos-5.x"></a>

### Unexpected LLVM Crash When Querying Doubles on CentOS 5.x

Certain queries involving `DOUBLE` columns could fail with a serious error. The fix
improves the generation of native machine instructions for certain chipsets.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-477](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-477)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title292"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--block-size-is-too-big-error-with-snappy-compressed-rcfile-containing-null"></a>

### "block size is too big" error with Snappy-compressed RCFile containing null

Queries could fail with a "block size is too big" error, due to `NULL` values in
RCFile tables using Snappy compression.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-482](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-482)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title293"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--cannot-query-rc-file-for-table-that-has-more-columns-than-the-data-file"></a>

### Cannot query RC file for table that has more columns than the data file

Queries could fail if an Impala RCFile table was defined with more columns than in the corresponding
RCFile data files.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-510](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-510)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title294"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--views-sometimes-not-utilizing-partition-pruning"></a>

### Views Sometimes Not Utilizing Partition Pruning

Certain combinations of clauses in a view definition for a partitioned table could result in inefficient
performance and incorrect results.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-495](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-495)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title295"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--update-the-serde-name-we-write-into-the-metastore-for-parquet-tables"></a>

### Update the serde name we write into the metastore for Parquet tables

The SerDes class string written into Parquet data files created by Impala was updated for compatibility
with Parquet support in Hive. See [Incompatible Changes Introduced in Impala 1.1.1](#impala_incompatible_changes--incompatible_changes_111)
for the steps to update older Parquet data files for Hive compatibility.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-485](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-485)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title296"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--selective-queries-over-large-tables-produce-unnecessary-memory-consumption"></a>

### Selective queries over large tables produce unnecessary memory consumption

A query returning a small result sets from a large table could tie up memory unnecessarily for the
duration of the query.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-534](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-534)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title297"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-stopped-to-query-avro-tables"></a>

### Impala stopped to query AVRO tables

Queries against Avro tables could fail depending on whether the Avro schema URL was specified in the
`TBLPROPERTIES` or `SERDEPROPERTIES` field. The fix causes Impala to check
both fields for the schema URL.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-538](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-538)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title298"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-continues-to-allocate-more-memory-even-though-it-has-exceed-its-mem-limit"></a>

### Impala continues to allocate more memory even though it has exceed its mem-limit

Queries could allocate substantially more memory than specified in the impalad
`-mem_limit` startup option. The fix causes more frequent checking of the limit during
query execution.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-520](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-520)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title299"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.1.0-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.1.0 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.1. For the full list of fixed issues, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title300"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--10-20-perf-regression-for-most-queries-across-all-table-formats"></a>

### 10-20% perf regression for most queries across all table formats

This issue is due to a performance tradeoff between systems running many queries concurrently, and
systems running a single query. Systems running only a single query could experience lower performance
than in early beta releases. Systems running many queries simultaneously should experience higher
performance than in the beta releases.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title301"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--planner-fails-with-join-requires-at-least-one-equality-predicate-between-the-two-tables-when-from-table-order-does-not-match-where-join-order"></a>

### planner fails with "Join requires at least one equality predicate between the two tables" when "from" table order does not match "where" join order

A query could fail if it involved 3 or more tables and the last join table was specified as a subquery.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-85](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-85)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title302"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--parquet-writer-uses-excessive-memory-with-partitions"></a>

### Parquet writer uses excessive memory with partitions

`INSERT` statements against partitioned tables using the Parquet format could use
excessive amounts of memory as the number of partitions grew large.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-257](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-257)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title303"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--comments-in-impala-shell-in-interactive-mode-are-not-handled-properly-causing-syntax-errors-or-wrong-results"></a>

### Comments in impala-shell in interactive mode are not handled properly causing syntax errors or wrong results

The impala-shell interpreter did not accept comment entered at the command line, making it problematic to copy and paste from scripts or other code examples.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-192](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-192)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title304"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--cancelled-queries-sometimes-aren-t-removed-from-the-inflight-query-list"></a>

### Cancelled queries sometimes aren't removed from the inflight query list

The Impala web UI would sometimes display a query as if it were still running, after the query was
cancelled.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-364](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-364)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title305"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-s-1.0.1-shell-broke-python-2.4-compatibility-attributeerror:-module-object-has-no-attribute-field_size_limit"></a>

### Impala's 1.0.1 Shell Broke Python 2.4 Compatibility (AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'field\_size\_limit)

The `impala-shell` command in Impala 1.0.1 does not work with Python 2.4, which is the
default on Red Hat 5.

For the `impala-shell` command in Impala 1.0, the `-o` option (pipe output
to a file) does not work with Python 2.4.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-396](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-396)

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title306"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.0.1-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.0.1 Release

This section lists the most significant issues fixed in Impala 1.0.1. For the full list of fixed issues, see [this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project%3Dimpala+and+fixVersion%3D%22Impala+1.0.1%22+and+resolution%3D%22Fixed%22).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title307"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-parquet-scanner-cannot-read-all-data-files-generated-by-other-frameworks"></a>

### Impala parquet scanner cannot read all data files generated by other frameworks

Impala might issue an erroneous error message when processing a Parquet data file produced by a
non-Impala Hadoop component.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-333](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-333)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title308"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-is-unable-to-query-rcfile-tables-which-describe-fewer-columns-than-the-file-s-header."></a>

### Impala is unable to query RCFile tables which describe fewer columns than the file's header.

If an RCFile table definition had fewer columns than the fields actually in the data files, queries would
fail.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-293](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-293)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title309"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-correctly-substitute-_host-with-hostname-in-principal"></a>

### Impala does not correctly substitute \_HOST with hostname in --principal

The `_HOST` placeholder in the `--principal` startup option was not
substituted with the correct hostname, potentially leading to a startup error in setups using Kerberos
authentication.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-351](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-351)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title310"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hbase-query-missed-the-last-region"></a>

### HBase query missed the last region

A query for an HBase table could omit data from the last region.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-356](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-356)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title311"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hbase-region-changes-are-not-handled-correctly"></a>

### Hbase region changes are not handled correctly

After a region in an HBase table was split or moved, an Impala query might return incomplete or
out-of-date results.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-300](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-300)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title312"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-state-for-successful-create-table-is-exception"></a>

### Query state for successful create table is EXCEPTION

After a successful `CREATE TABLE` statement, the corresponding query state would be
incorrectly reported as `EXCEPTION`.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-349](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-349)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title313"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--double-check-release-of-jni-allocated-byte-strings"></a>

### Double check release of JNI-allocated byte-strings

Operations involving calls to the Java JNI subsystem (for example, queries on HBase tables) could
allocate memory but not release it.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-358](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-358)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title314"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-returns-0-for-bad-time-values-in-unix_timestamp-hive-returns-null"></a>

### Impala returns 0 for bad time values in UNIX\_TIMESTAMP, Hive returns NULL

Impala returns 0 for bad time values in UNIX\_TIMESTAMP, Hive returns NULL.

**Impala:**

```
impala> select UNIX_TIMESTAMP('10:02:01') ;
impala> 0
```

**Hive:**

```
hive> select UNIX_TIMESTAMP('10:02:01') FROM tmp;
hive> NULL
```

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-16](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-16)

**Anticipated Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title315"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--insert-into-table-select-constant-does-not-work."></a>

### INSERT INTO TABLE SELECT <constant> does not work.

Insert INTO TABLE SELECT <constant> will not insert any data and may return an error.

**Anticipated Resolution**: Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title316"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-the-1.0-ga-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in the 1.0 GA Release

Here are the major user-visible issues fixed in Impala 1.0. For a full list of fixed issues, see
[this report in the Impala JIRA tracker](https://issues.apache.org/jira/issues/?jql=project+%3D+impala+AND+resolution+%3D+Fixed+AND+fixVersion+%3D+%22Impala+1.0%22+ORDER+BY+key+ASC%2C+assignee+ASC%2C+priority+DESC).

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title317"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--undeterministically-receive-error:-unknown-row-bach-destination...-and-error:-invalid-query-handle-from-impala-shell-when-running-union-query"></a>

### Undeterministically receive "ERROR: unknown row bach destination..." and "ERROR: Invalid query handle" from impala shell when running union query

A query containing both `UNION` and `LIMIT` clauses could intermittently
cause the `impalad` process to halt with a segmentation fault.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-183](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-183)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title318"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--insert-with-null-partition-keys-results-in-sigsegv."></a>

### Insert with NULL partition keys results in SIGSEGV.

An `INSERT` statement specifying a `NULL` value for one of the partitioning
columns could cause the `impalad` process to halt with a segmentation fault.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-190](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-190)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title319"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--insert-queries-don-t-show-completed-profiles-on-the-debug-webpage"></a>

### INSERT queries don't show completed profiles on the debug webpage

In the Impala web user interface, the profile page for an `INSERT` statement showed
obsolete information for the statement once it was complete.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-217](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-217)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title320"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-hbase-scan-is-very-slow"></a>

### Impala HBase scan is very slow

Queries involving an HBase table could be slower than expected, due to excessive memory usage on the
Impala nodes.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-231](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-231)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title321"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--add-some-library-version-validation-logic-to-impalad-when-loading-impala-lzo-shared-library"></a>

### Add some library version validation logic to impalad when loading impala-lzo shared library

No validation was done to check that the `impala-lzo` shared library was compatible with
the version of Impala, possibly leading to a crash when using LZO-compressed text files.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-234](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-234)

**Resolution:** Fixed

**Workaround:** Always upgrade the `impala-lzo` library at the same time as you upgrade
Impala itself.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title322"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--problems-inserting-into-tables-with-timestamp-partition-columns-leading-table-metadata-loading-failures-and-failed-dchecks"></a>

### Problems inserting into tables with TIMESTAMP partition columns leading table metadata loading failures and failed dchecks

`INSERT` statements for tables partitioned on columns involving datetime types could
appear to succeed, but cause errors for subsequent queries on those tables. The problem was especially
serious if an improperly formatted timestamp value was specified for the partition key.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-238](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-238)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title323"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ctrl-c-sometimes-interrupts-shell-in-system-call-rather-than-cancelling-query"></a>

### Ctrl-C sometimes interrupts shell in system call, rather than cancelling query

Pressing Ctrl-C in the `impala-shell` interpreter could sometimes display an error and
return control to the shell, making it impossible to cancel the query.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-243](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-243)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title324"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--empty-string-partition-value-causes-metastore-update-failure"></a>

### Empty string partition value causes metastore update failure

Specifying an empty string or `NULL` for a partition key in an `INSERT`
statement would fail.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-252](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-252)

**Resolution:** Fixed. The behavior for empty partition keys was made more compatible with the
corresponding Hive behavior.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title325"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--round-does-not-output-the-right-precision"></a>

### Round() does not output the right precision

The `round()` function did not always return the correct number of significant digits.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-266](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-266)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title326"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--cannot-cast-string-literal-to-string"></a>

### Cannot cast string literal to string

Casting from a string literal back to the same type would cause an "invalid type cast" error rather
than leaving the original value unchanged.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-267](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-267)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title327"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--excessive-mem-usage-for-certain-queries-which-are-very-selective"></a>

### Excessive mem usage for certain queries which are very selective

Some queries that returned very few rows experienced unnecessary memory usage.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-288](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-288)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title328"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--hdfsscannode-crashes-in-updatecounters"></a>

### HdfsScanNode crashes in UpdateCounters

A serious error could occur for relatively small and inexpensive queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-289](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-289)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title329"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--parquet-performance-issues-on-large-dataset"></a>

### Parquet performance issues on large dataset

Certain aggregation queries against Parquet tables were inefficient due to lower than required thread
utilization.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-292](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-292)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title330"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-not-populating-hive-metadata-correctly-for-create-table"></a>

### impala not populating hive metadata correctly for create table

The Impala `CREATE TABLE` command did not fill in the `owner` and
`tbl_type` columns in the Hive metastore database.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-295](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-295)

**Resolution:** Fixed. The metadata was made more Hive-compatible.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title331"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-daemons-die-if-statestore-goes-down"></a>

### impala daemons die if statestore goes down

The `impalad` instances in a cluster could halt when the `statestored`
process became unavailable.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-312](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-312)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title332"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--constant-select-clauses-do-not-work-in-subqueries"></a>

### Constant SELECT clauses do not work in subqueries

A subquery would fail if the `SELECT` statement inside it returned a constant value rather
than querying a table.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-67](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-67)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title333"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--right-outer-join-includes-nulls-as-well-and-hence-wrong-result-count"></a>

### Right outer Join includes NULLs as well and hence wrong result count

The result set from a right outer join query could include erroneous rows containing
`NULL` values.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-90](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-90)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title334"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--parquet-scanner-hangs-for-some-queries"></a>

### Parquet scanner hangs for some queries

The Parquet scanner non-deterministically hangs when executing some queries.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-204](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-204)

**Resolution:** Fixed

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title335"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.7-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.7 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title336"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-gracefully-handle-unsupported-hive-table-types-index-and-view-tables"></a>

### Impala does not gracefully handle unsupported Hive table types (INDEX and VIEW tables)

When attempting to load metadata from an unsupported Hive table type (INDEX and VIEW tables), Impala
fails with an unclear error message.

Bug:
[IMPALA-167](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-167)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title337"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ddl-statements-create-alter-drop-table-are-not-supported-in-the-impala-beta-release"></a>

### DDL statements (CREATE/ALTER/DROP TABLE) are not supported in the Impala Beta Release

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title338"></a>
