<a id="impala_fixed_issues--avro-is-not-supported-in-the-impala-beta-release"></a>

### Avro is not supported in the Impala Beta Release

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

**Workaround:** None

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title339"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-currently-allow-limiting-the-memory-consumption-of-a-single-query"></a>

### Impala does not currently allow limiting the memory consumption of a single query

It is currently not possible to limit the memory consumption of a single query. All tables on the right
hand side of JOIN statements need to be able to fit in memory. If they do not, Impala may crash due to
out of memory errors.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title340"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--aggregate-of-a-subquery-result-set-returns-wrong-results-if-the-subquery-contains-a-limit-and-data-is-distributed-across-multiple-nodes"></a>

### Aggregate of a subquery result set returns wrong results if the subquery contains a 'limit' and data is distributed across multiple nodes

Aggregate of a subquery result set returns wrong results if the subquery contains a 'limit' clause and
data is distributed across multiple nodes. From the query plan, it looks like we are just summing the
results from each worker node.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-20](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-20)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title341"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--partition-pruning-for-arbitrary-predicates-that-are-fully-bound-by-a-particular-partition-column"></a>

### Partition pruning for arbitrary predicates that are fully bound by a particular partition column

We currently cannot utilize a predicate like "country\_code in ('DE',
'FR', 'US')" to do partitioning pruning, because that requires an
equality predicate or a binary comparison.

We should create a superclass of planner.ValueRange, ValueSet, that can be constructed with an arbitrary
predicate, and whose isInRange(analyzer, valueExpr) constructs a literal predicate by substitution of the
valueExpr into the predicate.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-144](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-144)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.7

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title342"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.6-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.6 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title343"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-reads-the-namenode-address-and-port-as-command-line-parameters"></a>

### Impala reads the NameNode address and port as command line parameters

Impala reads the NameNode address and port as command line parameters rather than reading them from
`core-site.xml`. Updating the NameNode address in the `core-site.xml` file
does not propagate to Impala.

**Severity:** Low

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.6 - Impala reads the namenode location and port from the Hadoop
configuration files, though setting `-nn` and `-nn_port` overrides this.
Users are advised not to set `-nn` or `-nn_port`.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title344"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queries-may-fail-on-secure-environment-due-to-impalad-kerberos-ticket-expiration"></a>

### Queries may fail on secure environment due to impalad Kerberos ticket expiration

Queries may fail on secure environment due to `impalad` Kerberos tickets expiring. This
can happen if the Impala `-kerberos_reinit_interval` flag is set to a value ten minutes or
less. This may lead to an `impalad` requesting a ticket with a lifetime that is less than
the time to the next ticket renewal.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-64](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-64)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.6

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title345"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--concurrent-queries-may-fail-when-impala-uses-thrift-to-communicate-with-the-hive-metastore"></a>

### Concurrent queries may fail when Impala uses Thrift to communicate with the Hive Metastore

Concurrent queries may fail when Impala is using Thrift to communicate with part of the Hive Metastore
such as the Hive Metastore Service. In such a case, the error `get_fields failed: out of sequence
response"` may occur because Impala shared a single Hive Metastore Client connection across
threads. With Impala 0.6, a separate connection is used for each metadata request.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-48](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-48)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.6

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title346"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impalad-fails-to-start-if-unable-to-connect-to-the-hive-metastore"></a>

### impalad fails to start if unable to connect to the Hive Metastore

Impala fails to start if it is unable to establish a connection with the Hive Metastore. This behavior
was fixed, allowing Impala to start, even when no Metastore is available.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-58](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-58)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.6

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title347"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-treats-database-names-as-case-sensitive-in-some-contexts"></a>

### Impala treats database names as case-sensitive in some contexts

In some queries (including "USE database" statements), database names are treated as case-sensitive. This
may lead queries to fail with an IllegalStateException.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-44](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-44)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.6

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title348"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-does-not-ignore-hidden-hdfs-files"></a>

### Impala does not ignore hidden HDFS files

Impala does not ignore hidden HDFS files, meaning those files prefixed with a period '.' or underscore
'\_'. This diverges from Hive/MapReduce, which skips these files.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-18](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-18)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.6

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title349"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.5-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.5 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title350"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-may-have-reduced-performance-on-tables-that-contain-a-large-number-of-partitions"></a>

### Impala may have reduced performance on tables that contain a large number of partitions

Impala may have reduced performance on tables that contain a large number of partitions. This is due to
extra overhead reading/parsing the partition metadata.

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.5

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title351"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--backend-client-connections-not-getting-cached-causes-an-observable-latency-in-secure-clusters"></a>

### Backend client connections not getting cached causes an observable latency in secure clusters

Backend impalads do not cache connections to the coordinator. On a secure cluster, this introduces a
latency proportional to the number of backend clients involved in query execution, as the cost of
establishing a secure connection is much higher than in the non-secure case.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-38](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-38)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.5

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title352"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--concurrent-queries-may-fail-with-error:-table-object-has-not-been-been-initialised-:-partitions"></a>

### Concurrent queries may fail with error: "Table object has not been been initialised : `PARTITIONS`"

Concurrent queries may fail with error: `` "Table object has not been been initialised :
`PARTITIONS`" ``. This was due to a lack of locking in the Impala table/database metadata cache.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-30](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-30)

**Resolution**: Fixed in 0.5

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title353"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--unix_timestamp-format-behaviour-deviates-from-hive-when-format-matches-a-prefix-of-the-time-value"></a>

### UNIX\_TIMESTAMP format behaviour deviates from Hive when format matches a prefix of the time value

The Impala UNIX\_TIMESTAMP(val, format) operation compares the length of format and val and returns NULL
if they do not match. Hive instead effectively truncates val to the length of the format parameter.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-15](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-15)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.5

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title354"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.4-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.4 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title355"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-fails-to-refresh-the-hive-metastore-if-a-hive-temporary-configuration-file-is-removed"></a>

### Impala fails to refresh the Hive metastore if a Hive temporary configuration file is removed

Impala is impacted by Hive bug
[HIVE-3596](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HIVE-3596)
which may cause metastore refreshes to fail if a Hive temporary configuration file is deleted (normally
located at `/tmp/hive-<user>-<tmp_number>.xml`). Additionally, the
impala-shell will incorrectly report that the failed metadata refresh completed successfully.

**Anticipated Resolution:** To be fixed in a future release

**Workaround:** Restart the `impalad` service. Use the `impalad` log to
check for metadata refresh errors.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title356"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--lpad-rpad-builtin-functions-is-not-correct."></a>

### lpad/rpad builtin functions is not correct.

The lpad/rpad builtin functions generate the wrong results.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title357"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--files-with-.gz-extension-reported-as-not-supported"></a>

### Files with .gz extension reported as 'not supported'

Compressed files with extensions incorrectly generate an exception.

**Bug:**
[IMPALA-14](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/IMPALA-14)

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title358"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--queries-with-large-limits-would-hang."></a>

### Queries with large limits would hang.

Some queries with large limits were hanging.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title359"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--order-by-on-a-string-column-produces-incorrect-results-if-there-are-empty-strings"></a>

### Order by on a string column produces incorrect results if there are empty strings

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.4

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title360"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.3-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.3 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title361"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--all-table-loading-errors-show-as-unknown-table"></a>

### All table loading errors show as unknown table

If Impala is unable to load the metadata for a table for any reason, a subsequent query referring to that
table will return an `unknown table` error message, even if the table is known.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.3

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title362"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--a-table-that-cannot-be-loaded-will-disappear-from-show-tables"></a>

### A table that cannot be loaded will disappear from SHOW TABLES

After failing to load metadata for a table, Impala removes that table from the list of known tables
returned in `SHOW TABLES`. Subsequent attempts to query the table returns 'unknown table', even if the metadata for that table is fixed.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.3

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title363"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-cannot-read-from-hbase-tables-that-are-not-created-as-external-tables-in-the-hive-metastore."></a>

### Impala cannot read from HBase tables that are not created as external tables in the hive metastore.

Attempting to select from these tables fails.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.3

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title364"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--certain-queries-that-contain-outer-joins-may-return-incorrect-results"></a>

### Certain queries that contain OUTER JOINs may return incorrect results

Queries that contain OUTER JOINs may not return the correct results if there are predicates referencing
any of the joined tables in the WHERE clause.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.3.

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title365"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--issues-fixed-in-version-0.2-of-the-beta-release"></a>

## Issues Fixed in Version 0.2 of the Beta Release

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title366"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--subqueries-which-contain-aggregates-cannot-be-joined-with-other-tables-or-impala-may-crash"></a>

### Subqueries which contain aggregates cannot be joined with other tables or Impala may crash

Subqueries that contain an aggregate cannot be joined with another table or Impala may crash. For
example:

```
SELECT * FROM (SELECT sum(col1) FROM some_table GROUP BY col1) t1 JOIN other_table ON (...);
```

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.2

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title367"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--an-insert-with-a-limit-that-runs-as-more-than-one-query-fragment-inserts-more-rows-than-the-limit."></a>

### An insert with a limit that runs as more than one query fragment inserts more rows than the limit.

For example:

```
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE test SELECT * FROM test2 LIMIT 1;
```

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.2

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title368"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--query-with-limit-clause-might-fail."></a>

### Query with limit clause might fail.

For example:

```
SELECT * FROM test2 LIMIT 1;
```

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.2

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title369"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--files-in-unsupported-compression-formats-are-read-as-plain-text."></a>

### Files in unsupported compression formats are read as plain text.

Attempting to read such files does not generate a diagnostic.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.2

<a id="impala_fixed_issues--ariaid-title370"></a>
<a id="impala_fixed_issues--impala-server-raises-a-null-pointer-exception-when-running-an-hbase-query."></a>

### Impala server raises a null pointer exception when running an HBase query.

When querying an HBase table whose row-key is string type, the Impala server may raise a null pointer
exception.

**Resolution:** Fixed in 0.2
